title: Controller, Technical Accounting & Operations
description:
**Controller**

Controller, Technical Accounting \& Operations

* ***This role is intended to replace the owner as the final escalation point for accounting****.*
* ***If you prefer to stay at a high level and avoid hands\-on accounting work, this role will not be a fit.***

We are seeking a **high\-level accounting leader** who can own technical accounting decisions, elevate team performance, and build scalable processes across a growing client services environment.

***This is not a traditional Controller role.***

**Our Approach to Accounting (Read This Before Applying)**

We are a roll\-up\-your\-sleeves team.

Even at the Controller level, this role is expected to:

* Step into messy or incomplete books and **personally clean them up**
* Perform **hands\-on accounting work when needed**, including reconciliations and transaction\-level review
* Build **clear, repeatable processes and SOPs** from that work
* Train and mentor team members to successfully take over those processes

We believe the best accounting leaders:

* Are not above the work
* Lead by example
* Build systems by doing—not just directing

**This role is not for someone who only wants to review or delegate.**

It is for someone who:  
**Fixes**  
**Builds**  
**Documents**  
**Teaches**  
Then elevates others

You will serve as the **technical authority for accounting**, while also improving how our team executes—ensuring financials are accurate, timely, and consistently delivered across multiple clients.

**Location:** North Loop, Minneapolis, MN (Twin Cities Metro Area)

**What You’ll Own** Technical Accounting Leadership

* **Fix, build, document, teach, then evaluate**
* Serve as the **final reviewer** for complex accounting matters across multiple clients
* Resolve technical accounting issues (accruals, revenue recognition, multi\-entity structures, cleanup work, etc.)
* Ensure financial statements are **accurate, compliant, and decision\-ready**
* Act as the **escalation point** for accounting questions—removing the need for owner involvement

Client Onboarding \& Cleanup

* Lead onboarding of new clients, including **cleanup and restructuring of books**
* Identify issues quickly and implement **clean, scalable solutions**
* Establish consistent accounting standards across clients

Process \& Operational Excellence

* Build and refine **month\-end close processes, workflows, and checklists**
* Improve team execution through **clear timelines, accountability, and structure**
* Implement systems that ensure **consistency across multiple clients and teams**
* Drive continuous improvement in how accounting is delivered

Team Leadership \& Development

* Review work from bookkeepers, senior accountants, and controllers
* Provide coaching and feedback to improve **accuracy, efficiency, and confidence**
* Set clear expectations and hold the team accountable to deadlines and standards
* Lead weekly huddles and monthly reviews to keep work on track

**What We’re Looking For**

Required:

* 10\+ years of accounting experience, including **Controller or senior\-level roles**
* Strong technical accounting knowledge (GAAP, accruals, financial reporting, cleanup work)
* Experience reviewing and correcting the work of other accountants
* Ability to **solve complex accounting issues independently**
* Experience working across multiple entities, clients, or business units
* Strong QuickBooks experience (Online preferred)

Strongly Preferred:

* CPA license
* Master’s degree in Accounting or related field
* Experience in a **client service, outsourced, or multi\-entity environment**

**Who You Are**

* You are a **technical accounting expert who is not afraid to get into the details**
* You are comfortable stepping into **bookkeeping and cleanup work when needed** to understand and fix issues at the root
* You believe in **building processes by doing the work first**, then documenting and delegating
* You have a strong **sense of ownership and urgency**—you don’t wait for direction, you move work forward
* You know how to **prioritize across multiple clients, deadlines, and competing priorities**
* You bring a **methodical approach to execution**, creating clear workflows, timelines, and deliverables
* You take pride in developing others and can **train bookkeepers and accountants to take over your work successfully**
* You hold high standards for accuracy and accountability—both for yourself and your team
* You are not driven by title or hierarchy—you are driven by **outcomes, quality, and team success**

**Why You’ll Love Working at Owl**

* **Hybrid flexibility** – work from our North Loop office and onsite with local clients.
* **Supportive, growth\-oriented team culture.**
* **Career development opportunities** in client strategy and leadership.
* **Paid parking, free breakfast and snacks, onsite gym, 401(k) match, tiered PTO, and generous benefits with disability, life, medical, dental and more.**
* **Work/life balance**

If you’re looking for a place where your expertise matters — where you can **mentor others, advise clients, and shape the financial health of growing businesses** — apply today!

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) 5% Match
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* On\-site gym
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Application Question(s):

* Have you worked in an outsourced, client\-service, or fractional accounting environment before?
* Are you able to work in a hybrid role based in the Twin Cities metro area (North loop office and local client visits)? This is not remote work. Applicants need to live in the Twin Cities and spend time between our co\-working space and client sites.
* How comfortable are you managing multiple clients or entities simultaneously? Which task management tools do you use to help you?
* Describe your experience managing or mentoring accounting or bookkeeping staff. How do you hold staff accountable to objectives?

Education:

* Master's (Preferred)

Experience:

* Accounting: 10 years (Required)
* Controller or Senior Accounting: 10 years (Required)
* QuickBooks: 5 years (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Accounting Specialist
description:
**About Us**

Erickson Outdoor Lighting is a locally owned and rapidly growing design\-build company specializing in high\-end landscape lighting and outdoor environments. We take pride in craftsmanship, innovation, and customer experience — from concept through installation. With projects spanning residential estates, lake homes, and commercial properties across the Twin Cities, our team combines design creativity with technical precision.

We are seeking an experienced Accounting Specialist to lead our accounting operations, streamline processes, and provide clear financial insight that supports smart, strategic decisions. This is an opportunity to take ownership of a well\-established, growing business’ financial systems and help shape its next stage of growth.

**About the Role**

This in\-office role goes beyond traditional bookkeeping. You’ll be the financial backbone of the company — managing accounting operations, overseeing job costing and budgets, and preparing timely, accurate reports that guide both day\-to\-day and long\-term decisions. You’ll work directly with ownership and collaborate with our fractional CFO and leadership team.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Manage all daily accounting functions, including AP, AR, payroll entries, and bank reconciliations using Quickbooks Online.
* Maintain and reconcile the general ledger, ensuring clean, accurate records.
* Oversee job costing and profitability tracking for outdoor lighting and installation projects.
* Prepare and analyze monthly financial statements, budgets, and forecasts.
* Support leadership with key financial insights, KPIs, and performance reporting.
* Coordinate with our fractional CFO and external CPA for tax and strategic planning.
* Continuously improve systems and workflows to increase efficiency and accuracy.
* Manage vendor payments, customer invoicing, and expense tracking.

**Qualifications**

* Strong proficiency in QuickBooks Online and Excel.
* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field preferred.
* 5\+ years of progressive accounting or bookkeeping experience.
* Experience in construction, landscaping, or project\-based businesses strongly preferred.
* Deep understanding of job costing, budgeting, and project accounting.
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Clear communicator with the ability to collaborate across departments.
* Self\-starter who thrives in a fast\-paced, growing company.

**What We Offer**

* Competitive salary based on experience.
* Direct collaboration with company leadership and the CFO.
* Opportunity to build and lead the accounting function within a thriving organization.
* Supportive, family\-oriented culture that values trust, accountability, and growth.
* Flexible scheduling and potential hybrid work options after onboarding.

Compensation: Commensurate with experience

Full time, on\-site at our office in Mound, MN

Job Type: Full\-time

Base Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $80,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

title: Fractional CFO/Controller, Non-Profit
description:
**Fractional CFO/Controller, Non\-Profit** 

**Location:** Utah

**Company Overview**

Rock CFO is Fractional CFO Firm that started in Utah in 2019\. They have a Fractional CFO team and have expanded operations to 2 locations, Utah and Kansas City, MO. They serve clients typically in the $5M to $50M range.

**Summary of Role**

We have an immediate opening for a Fractional CFO or Controller to work with the Rock CFO team to help a non\-profit organization improve their accounting team and processes. They need assistance passing an audit and getting some things organized. They can also use some experienced guidance and leadership on seeing how to become a stronger Finance and Accounting team that efficiently runs a tight shop.

You will have the flexibility of working 1 to 2 days per week and add great value to this team and project. You will get to be hands on with some of the cleanup and more importantly drive the vision and help mentor the team on how they can build a top\-notch accounting and finance organization. You will report to the President and the be peers with the current leader of the accounting function. Although, some mentorship for this accounting leader will be helpful.

**Responsibilities**

**1\. Oversee cleanup of books**

* **Audit prep**. The team is still working toward preparing for and passing the annual audit. Some work will need to be done and spearheaded to incorporate this work.
* **Vision setting**: Help the team see how to take the Accounting and Finance functions to the next level of proficiency and dependability for the organization. Help them build and achieve this next iteration of their teams growth.

**Systems and processes**

* **New Accounting system transition:** The team has begun the journey to transition to a new accounting system. They need some resources and guidance if fully implementing and adopting the new system.

**Finance and Budget**

* **Grant Accounting, budgeting, and forecasting:** Improve timeliness of grant accounting, forecasting, and budgeting. Work with division leaders to understand amount spent, amount left to spend in budgets.

**Qualifications**

· 25 years or more of accounting work experience

· 5\+ years working in a non\-profit environment receiving Federal and State Grants.

· Ability to lead a clean up process and build a successful accounting team.

Pay: $4,000\.00 \- $7,500\.00 per month

Work Location: In person
company_description: Rock CFO provides Fractional CFO services for businesses in the $5M to $50M range. We also provide flat rate full-time recruiting services for Finance and Accounting positions.

title: Part-Time Controller / Senior Accountant
description:
**Wealth Management Firm — Bloomfield Hills, MI**  
**Hybrid \| 2 Days in Office Weekly**

A well\-established **Bloomfield Hills wealth management firm** is seeking a **Part\-Time Controller / Senior Accountant** to oversee day\-to\-day accounting operations and serve as the primary internal accounting lead.

This position will replace a current fractional accounting resource and is ideal for an experienced accountant who wants meaningful ownership and leadership responsibility without a full\-time schedule.

You will work closely with the firm’s **CFO** and external **CPA**, helping ensure accurate financial reporting, strong processes, and smooth accounting operations across the organization.

**Position Summary**

This role owns the accounting function for a small professional services team. The ideal candidate is comfortable operating both strategically and tactically — able to manage financial processes while also executing core accounting work when needed.

**Schedule**

● Part\-time (approximately 16–20 hours/week)

● **In\-office in Bloomfield Hills two days per week**

● Remaining hours (as needed) flexible/remote

**Engagement Type**

● Part\-time employee **or** independent contractor

**Key ResponsibilitiesAccounting Leadership \& Oversight**

● Serve as the firm’s primary accounting lead

● Maintain accuracy and integrity of the general ledger

● Manage monthly, quarterly, and year\-end close processes

● Prepare and review financial statements and supporting schedules. Co\-ordinate

with parent company representatives on financial reporting and issues.

● Partner with the CFO on financial insights and reporting needs

● Coordinate with external CPA for tax filings and compliance

**Accounts Payable \& Accounts Receivable**

● **Oversee and execute AP and AR processes**

● Post invoicing, cash applications and monitor A/R

● Manage vendor payments and expense tracking

● Maintain strong documentation and approval workflows

● Monitor cash flow and receivable aging

**Financial Operations**

● Record journal entries, accruals, and adjustments

● Perform account reconciliations

● Maintain accounting records within **Great Plains**

● Support budgeting, forecasting, and analysis initiatives

**Payroll and Human Resource Functions**

● Manage Semi\-monthly payroll with ADP, process annual and special bonuses

● Process 401k, HSA and FSA contributions and annual form 5500\.

● Manage health, disability and life insurance renewals

● Onboard new hires and maintain employee records

**Qualifications**

● 5\+ years of progressive accounting experience

● Experience operating at a **Senior Accountant or Controller level**

● Strong GAAP knowledge

● Experience owning accounting operations in a small or mid\-sized organization

● Comfortable handling both oversight **and hands\-on execution**

● Experience with **Great Plains** and/or Workday preferred

● Strong organizational and communication skills

● High level of independence and accountability

**Ideal Candidate \-** Youmay be a great fit if you:

● Previously worked in public accounting or as a Controller/Senior Accountant

● Want meaningful responsibility without a full\-time role

● Enjoy being the go\-to accounting professional inside an organization

● Are comfortable rolling up your sleeves in a small\-team environment

● Appreciate working directly with leadership in a collaborative setting

**Work Environment**

● Professional wealth management office

● Collaborative small team

● Stable organization with established leadership

● Long\-term opportunity with flexibility

**Compensation**

Competitive and commensurate with experience. Structured based on employee or contractor engagement.

Pay: $45\.00 \- $75\.00 per hour

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Bloomfield Hills, MI 48304

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**Overview**   
We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to partner with organizations seeking expert financial leadership on a flexible, part\-time basis. The ideal candidate will bring a wealth of experience in financial management, corporate accounting, and strategic planning to help organizations optimize their financial health, ensure compliance, and accelerate growth. This role offers an exciting opportunity to influence key business decisions, oversee complex financial operations, and provide high\-level guidance across diverse industries. As a vital member of the leadership team, you will leverage your expertise to drive fiscal discipline, enhance risk management practices, and support sustainable development.

**Duties**

* Develop and implement comprehensive financial strategies aligned with organizational goals to promote growth and stability.
* Oversee corporate accounting functions, including financial reporting, regulatory compliance (such as GAAP, IFRS, IAS), and adherence to SOX controls.
* Lead risk analysis initiatives to identify potential financial threats and develop mitigation plans.
* Manage treasury functions including cash flow forecasting, banking relationships, and liquidity management.
* Support mergers \& acquisitions activities through due diligence, valuation analysis, negotiation support, and integration planning.
* Prepare detailed financial reports and interpret complex data for senior leadership to inform decision\-making.
* Conduct market analysis and business evaluations to identify opportunities for expansion or cost optimization.
* Supervise budgeting processes, expense management, cost control measures, and profit \& loss analysis.
* Ensure compliance with regulatory reporting requirements and maintain internal controls based on frameworks like COSO.
* Provide leadership in implementing advanced financial software tools and systems for efficient data management.
* Collaborate with management on strategic planning initiatives that enhance revenue management and overall fiscal health.

**Requirements**

* Proven experience as a CFO or senior finance executive with a focus on corporate finance, governmental accounting, or non\-profit sectors.
* Strong understanding of financial concepts including risk management, financial analysis, forecasting, and cost control.
* Extensive knowledge of accounting standards such as GAAP, IFRS, IAS; familiarity with ASC 606 is a plus.
* Demonstrated expertise in SOX compliance, regulatory reporting, and internal control frameworks like COSO.
* Skilled in financial modeling, business analysis, and market evaluation techniques.
* Experience managing treasury functions including cash flow forecasting and banking relationships.
* Excellent leadership skills with the ability to supervise teams and influence senior leadership decision\-making.
* Proficiency in financial software systems coupled with technical accounting expertise in areas such as tax accounting and cost accounting.
* Strong negotiation skills paired with strategic thinking for business development opportunities like mergers \& acquisitions.
* Exceptional analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex financial data into actionable insights.
* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting or related field; advanced certifications such as CPA or CFA are preferred but not required. Join us as a Fractional CFO to shape the future of organizations by delivering expert financial stewardship that drives success!

Pay: $85,000\.00 \- $130,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Controller
description:
Modern\-Amenities  
About Us  


Modern\-Amenities is a rapidly expanding entrepreneurial company operating at the intersection of physical infrastructure, digital innovation, and business enablement. We're in a hyper\-growth phase, managing a diverse portfolio of 3\-4 thriving operating companies that are reshaping their respective industries:  


Modern Amenities: A B2B micro market leader serving 10,000\+ locations nationwide  


VendHub: An innovative ecommerce platform empowering vending operators  


Breakthrough Sales: High\-performance in\-person sales teams  


AIMS (AI\-Managing Services): A forward\-thinking marketing agency leveraging cutting\-edge technology  


We're in a hyper growth stage with a very healthy financial state. This is your opportunity to join a dynamic, founder\-led company during its most exciting growth phase and build the financial infrastructure that will scale us to the next level.  


The Opportunity  


We're looking for an Controller who thrives in fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environments and wants to make a real impact. This isn't your typical corporate controller role—you'll have the autonomy to identify problems, implement solutions, and build processes from the ground up across multiple entities. You'll work directly with leadership, including our fractional CFO, and play a critical role in professionalizing our financial operations while maintaining the agility that got us here.  


If you're tired of being a cog in a corporate machine and want to see the direct impact of your work while gaining exposure to diverse business models, this role is for you.  


What You'll Do  


Financial Oversight \& Quality Control  


Oversee and review work from our external bookkeeper, ensuring accurate transaction coding and catching errors before they compound across multiple entities  


Serve as the quality control layer between day\-to\-day bookkeeping and executive financial reporting  


Full\-Cycle Controller Operations  


Own the complete AP cycle: invoice processing, vendor management, and bill pay across all entities  


Own the complete AR cycle: invoicing, collections, and customer inquiries  


Oversee and manage intercompany processes across our company structure, ensuring accurate eliminations and reliable consolidated financial reporting.  


Month\-End Close \& Reporting  


Lead month\-end close processes, including reconciliations, accruals, and journal entries  


Prepare timely, accurate financial statements for each operating entity and consolidated reports  


Collaborate with our fractional CFO on management reporting and ad\-hoc financial analysis  


Process Improvement \& Systems  


Identify process gaps and implement improvements—you'll have the autonomy to fix what's broken  


Build scalable accounting processes that can support our continued growth  


Enhance our QuickBooks Online infrastructure to support multi\-entity operations  


What You Bring  


Required Qualifications  


3\-6 years of controller experience, preferably in dynamic or multi\-entity environments  


QuickBooks Online proficiency (you should be able to train others)  


Demonstrated experience with intercompany transactions and multi\-entity environments  


Proven ability to work independently and take ownership of processes end\-to\-end  


Strong attention to detail with an investigative mindset—you don't just record transactions, you understand them  


Self\-starter mentality with the ability to thrive in an environment where processes are still being built  


Nice to Have  


Experience in a growth\-stage company or building/improving accounting processes from scratch  


CPA or CPA candidate  


Experience supervising bookkeepers or junior accounting staff  


Familiarity with process documentation and internal controls  


Growth mindset with aspirations of becoming a Controller as we scale  


Why Join Us?  


Real Impact: Your work directly influences business decisions and company trajectory  


Autonomy: We trust you to identify problems and implement solutions without bureaucracy  


Learning: Gain exposure to multiple business models and industries simultaneously  


Growth: As we scale, you'll scale with us—with clear paths to Controller and beyond  


Culture: Work with a founder\-led team that values entrepreneurial thinking and execution  


Stability Meets Excitement: We have the financial backing and infrastructure of an established business with the energy and opportunity of a startup  


Compensation \& Benefits  


Base Salary: $95,000 \- $115,000 (commensurate with experience)  


Plus: Comprehensive benefits package  


Location  


Coburg, Oregon  


Ready to build something significant? If you're an controller professional who wants more than just a job—if you want to be part of building the financial foundation of a rapidly growing company—we want to hear from you.  


Modern\-Amenities is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Associate Director, Private Equity/Hedge Fund Controller
description:
**Position Overview:**



The Associate Director provides fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO) support to hedge fund and private equity clients while overseeing key fund administration and accounting processes. In this role, you will review fund NAV calculations, oversee financial reporting, coordinate audits and tax processes, and serve as a primary liaison between clients and fund administrators. The position requires a detail\-oriented professional who can manage multiple client engagements while ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and strong operational oversight.



This is an excellent opportunity for a finance professional with experience in private funds who is looking to deepen their expertise in fund accounting and operations while building trusted client relationships. The role offers exposure to a wide range of fund structures and investment strategies while contributing to process improvements and the continued growth of the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Services team.


**About PINE:**



PINE is a dynamic and growing organization that provides institutional\-quality outsourced solutions for funds and investment managers. Our team is driven by the belief that we are contributing to something remarkable.



At PINE, we prioritize client service and collaborative culture. The ideal candidate will be motivated, detail\-oriented, eager to learn, and thrive in a high\-performing and supportive environment. Our culture is built around an "all for one and one for all" philosophy. Your contributions matter, and we are a team that embraces challenges and believes in growing through doing.


**Responsibilities**


* Nurture multiple client relationships
* Review the monthly/quarterly calculation of hedge fund/private equity fund NAVs as prepared by the administrator.
* Track, process, review and approve fund expenses
* Review partner allocations and waterfall schedules, and fee calculations including, but not limited to, management fees, professional fees, and performance fees.
* Review capital activity including capital calls, distributions, and transfers
* Coordinate and manage the external audit and tax process on behalf of multiple clients, including managing the preparation and issuance of audited financial statements and reviewing and approving annual K\-1s for private funds and tax returns.
* Provide oversight of clients' management company books and records including payroll processing, accounts payable and receivable, general bookkeeping and invoicing, 1099 preparation, and annual budgeting.
* Work closely with colleagues across engagements, providing insights and support to ensure accuracy, efficiency, and client satisfaction. The successful candidate will confidently review and challenge fund administrator work to ensure accuracy, GAAP compliance, and timeliness.
* Spearhead the development and implementation of streamlined processes and procedures within the CFO Services department, establish best practices and standard operating procedures, and utilize data\-driven insights to evaluate and enhance operational efficiency and scalability.


**Qualifications**


* 6\-12 years of financial services experience (Accounting/Audit/Administration/Hedge funds/Private Equity Funds)
* Must have experience with private equity funds or hedge funds and be able to review NAV calculations, management fees/incentive fees/carry calculations and year end financial statements
* The ideal candidate should possess strong accounting knowledge and technical expertise, with the ability to interpret complex transactions and apply U.S. GAAP and industry\-specific guidance to private funds.
* Proven experience in financial statement preparation and review, with a deep understanding of fair value measurements, consolidation considerations, and investor allocations.
* Previous asset management or pooled vehicle experience, including exposure to asset classes such as fixed income, public and private equities, and derivatives.
* Demonstrated ability to identify risks and control gaps in processes and successfully delivering process improvement
* Understanding of private fund offering docs and the intricacies of private equity vehicles
* Excellent verbal, written and presentation skills are essential
* Strong organizational skills with attention to deadlines
* Proficient in the use of MS Office: strong knowledge of Excel and Word required
* Proficient in QuickBooks
* Passion for delivering an exceptional client experience


**Work Model (Remote/Hybrid)**


* Candidates located in the Denver area who are able to follow a hybrid schedule working from our Denver office 3 days each week are preferred.
* Open to hiring remote employees located in the US with a minimum of 6 years of relevant experience.
* Some periodic travel to Denver will be expected for remote employees.


 **Salary:** $80,000 \- $120,000



Actual compensation will be determined based on experience, qualifications, and other job\-related factors. This position may also be eligible for discretionary bonus compensation.


**Employee Benefits**


* Health Insurance – we cover 100% of the cost for employees
* A comprehensive selection of medical, dental, and vision health plans
* Life and disability insurance
* 401(k) savings plan
* Unlimited vacation policy
* Paid maternity and paternity leave


**What You Can Expect From PINE**


**Work\-life Balance**


* We trust you to be the expert on creating an environment that allows you to bring your best self to work. With unlimited vacation and flexible work\-from\-home options, we encourage a work\-life balance that brings out the best in our team.


**Be a Leader**


* At PINE, we prioritize ensuring that each team member has the chance to emerge as a leader and make substantial contributions to our company.


**Experience an Environment of Learning**


* We place an emphasis on learning from each other and celebrating our unique expertise. There are abundant opportunities to learn and hone new skills.
  


PINE Advisor Solutions provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.



This position is expected to remain open for approximately two weeks. The posting may close sooner if the position is filled or if a sufficient number of qualified applications are received.

title: Financial Controller
description:
**Controller**

**Role Summary**

The Controller owns Sivo’s day\-to\-day financial operations, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and insight across all accounting activities. This is a ***hands\-on*** role where you’ll reconcile, invoice, forecast, and report. You’ll also play a key role in providing leadership with data\-driven visibility into the company’s financial health and future. **Note: This role is a financial team of 1 with oversight from a Fractional CFO. It is all hands\-on. There are no direct reports for this role.** 

*This role requires that you live in the Minneapolis\-St. Paul, MN area.* 

**Key responsibilities**

Your core responsibilities will include:

· Running the financial reporting, compliance, and planning.

· Managing hands\-on, all day\-to\-day accounting operations, including **Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable** which includes direct follow\-up with clients.

· Partnering with leadership to ensure financial strategy supports business strategy.

· Developing efficient systems and tools for reporting, forecasting, and expense management.

· Providing proactive insights and recommendations to strengthen decision\-making.

· Enter and ensure Payroll is calculated and processed correctly.

· Creating monthly and yearly P \&L

· Managing company expenses and budgets

· Managing vendor relationships with our bank, insurance broker, HR platform/payroll provider, CPA, different states for taxes, and unemployment (with HR support).

· Maintain and reconcile QuickBooks (online version) accounts, ensuring accuracy and completeness

· Prepare and process client invoices, track collections, and manage vendor/talent payments

· Oversee monthly closes, journal entries, and reconciliations

· Produce cash flow reports, forecasts, and variance analyses for leadership review

· Partner with the COO on budgeting, forecasting, and scenario planning

· Maintain compliance with tax, payroll, and other regulatory requirements (with external CPA support)

· Implement and refine internal financial processes, controls, and documentation

**What Success Looks Like**

* Clean, timely, and accurate books that are always ready for review or reporting
* Reliable cash flow visibility and financial forecasting that drives confident decisions
* Smooth, predictable invoicing and vendor payment cycles
* A reputation as the calm, trusted voice of financial clarity across the company

Pay: $75,000\.00 \- $90,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Work from home

Application Question(s):

* This role is hand\-on doing all of the financial work with no team members, is this what you are looking for?

Experience:

* Corporate finance: 7 years (Required)

Location:

* Minneapolis\-Saint Paul, MN (Required)

title: Fractional Financial Controller/CFO
description:
Fractional Financial Controller (Part\-Time / or Full\-Time Contract)

Purpose of the role  
Provide financial oversight and ensure accurate billing, reporting, and internal controls while supervising the accounting workflow. The role supports the existing team and ensures invoices, financial statements, and job costing are accurate until internal staff are fully trained.

Reporting  
Reports directly to the owner / management.

Scope of the role  
Part\-time engagement, typically one day per week or approximately 8–40 hours per week.

Key responsibilities

Financial oversight  
Oversee the accounting process and ensure accuracy of financial records.  
Review general ledger entries and maintain proper accounting controls.  
Prepare or review monthly financial statements and management reports.  
Coordinate with the company’s CPA for year\-end reporting and tax preparation.

Billing and invoicing  
Review invoices before they are issued to customers to ensure accuracy.  
Ensure rental billing follows the company’s 28\-day billing cycle.  
Confirm billing includes correct charges for freight, installation, dismantling, rental, and tax.  
Work with operations and sales to ensure billing aligns with quotes and contracts.

Accounts receivable  
Review AR aging reports.  
Work with the AR team member to improve collections and reduce overdue balances.  
Monitor customer credit exposure.

Accounts payable oversight  
Review AP process and approve payment runs when required.  
Ensure vendor invoices are coded correctly.

Job costing and project review  
Review project billing against job records and work orders.  
Identify missing charges or billing discrepancies.  
Ensure accurate job profitability reporting.

Staff support and training  
Provide guidance to accounting staff responsible for AP, AR, and invoicing.  
Train staff member responsible for invoicing to ensure billing accuracy and consistency.

Process improvement  
Implement procedures and controls for billing, job costing, and financial reporting.  
Improve financial visibility for management through clear reporting and dashboards.

Preferred qualifications

Experience as a controller or senior accounting manager.  
Strong knowledge of construction, equipment rental, or job\-cost accounting preferred.  
Experience with accounting systems and financial reporting.  
CPA or equivalent experience preferred but not required.

Typical engagement terms

Full\-time or Part\-time contract role.  
Minimum of estimated 8–40 hours per week.

Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time, Contract

Pay: $70,749\.52 \- $85,203\.72 per year

Expected hours: 8 – 40 per week

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Education:

* Bachelor's (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

title: CFO/Senior Controller/FP&A Manager
description:
At ICI Alliance, we are a high\-performing team of esteemed financial professionals who streamline financial strategy and empower business owners through education, reporting, and insights, to rapidly make efficient and informed decisions for their businesses.

The Finance Professional is an essential role in our collaborative, encouraging, and growth\-minded organization. As an influential leader and directly interfacing with clients as the financial\-strategy expert, you will have multiple opportunities to influence, impact, and thrive as a key contributor.

Reporting to the CEO, you will be empowered to bring your ideas, share your insights, and make decisions that impact the client base. Solidifying our commitment to results, empowerment, and maximizing financial impact.

ESSENTIAL COMPONENTS OF YOUR CONTRIBUTIONS

* Financial Planning \& Analysis (FP\&A) – Providing in\-depth financial modeling, cash flow forecasting, and strategic insights to empower business owners and directly influence their organization’s sustainable growth
* Expertly Educating \& Advising – Ensuring your clients have full visibility and knowledge into our service opportunities to enhance their business by expanding services for them
* Ensuring Compliance \& Best Practices – Maintaining and continuously improving your strong financial acumen, applying GAAP principles while optimizing financial processes with the clients
* Curating Phenomenal Client Relationships – Delighting the customer as the dedicated and trusted primary point of contact for multiple clients, delivering CFO\-level insights, financial modeling, and strategic guidance
* Commitment to our Mission – Actively seeking new clients because of your passion for our mission in streamlining financial strategy and empowering business owners

EXPERIENTIAL SUCCESS CHARACTERISTICS

A people\-first leader.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to pragmatically and logically group information, assess information, and relay information.

Exceptional customer service skills, including demonstrating care and concern for client interests and concerns, effectively receiving feedback and sharing feedback, listening, hearing, understanding, and active and responsive communication.

Strategic financial thinker with deep knowledge of GAAP, FP\&A, and financial modeling.

Ability to energetically problem\-solve and identify timely resolutions

Identifies with the excitement of a high\-growth entrepreneurial environment.

Motivational leader with established professionally\-trained experience managing and developing financial professionals.

The ideal candidate will have at least 7 to 10 years of progressive accounting experience.

DIFFERENTIATED CAREER OPPORTUNITY

Uncapped Financial Earnings Potential – Income grows as you grow your client base, upsell services, and optimize your team’s performance

True Entrepreneurial Freedom – The CFO thinks like a business owner and therefore is fully capable of identifying opportunities to drive value to the client and increase profit margins

Influential Leadership \& Enhanced Earning Potential – The CFO will lead a P.O.D. of financial professionals, providing the opportunity to scale your impact and earnings

Client\-Centric Approach – The CFO thrives in a highly visible role, interacting directly with multiple clients and providing strategic financial guidance

Human Connection through Networking – The CFO is encouraged to meet and consistently interact with leaders and executives of multiple organizations, networks, and associations

WHY THIS CAREER OPPORTUNITY IS RIGHT FOR YOU

✅ Entrepreneurial Culture – We expect you to think and act like an owner

✅ High\-Performance Team – Work with top\-tier financial professionals

✅ Scalable Earnings – Your success determines your compensation

✅ Work\-Life Flexibility – Hybrid work schedule \& location

Earnings Potential – Part\-Time / Fractional CFO

* This is a performance\-based, part\-time role paid as a percentage of revenue from your assigned clients.
* Commission rewards both new client growth and ongoing management.
* As the client base grows, there may be an option for a full\-time salary in addition to performance\-based pay.

DISCOVER MORE Here’s how you can reach us to find out more about this incredible career opportunity \- email hr@icialliance.com

Pay: $36,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Salt Lake City, UT 84109

title: Fractional Controller
description:
**Company Overview**

**Kava Culture** is a multi\-entity organization operating a growing kava bar franchise (28\+ locations across multiple states) alongside our sister company, **Botanical Brewing Co.**, a beverage manufacturing and wholesale business. Together, we operate across hospitality, franchising, and CPG manufacturing with increasing complexity and scale.

**Position Summary**

We are seeking a **Fractional Controller** to provide senior\-level accounting oversight, financial control, and close accountability across all entities. This role is responsible for ensuring the books are **accurate, timely, compliant, and decision\-ready**, while working closely with an internal Senior Accountant and external CPA firm.

This is a **hands\-on oversight role**, not a CFO or strategic finance position. The Controller will review, correct, and enforce accounting standards, close discipline, and internal controls in a multi\-entity, multi\-location environment.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Accounting Oversight \& Close Ownership**

* Own and oversee the **monthly close process** across all entities
* Review and approve financial statements prior to leadership distribution
* Ensure accuracy of P\&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow statements
* Enforce close deadlines, checklists, and documentation standards
* Identify and correct accounting errors, misclassifications, and inconsistencies

**Supervision of Senior Accountant**

* Review reconciliations, journal entries, and schedules prepared by Senior Accountant
* Provide guidance on complex accounting issues and edge cases
* Set expectations for accuracy, timelines, and documentation
* Serve as escalation point for accounting judgment calls

**Multi\-Entity \& Intercompany Accounting**

* Oversee intercompany allocations, eliminations, and reconciliations
* Ensure proper accounting for franchise fees, royalties, brand funds, and reimbursements
* Validate manufacturing\-to\-corporate and wholesale transactions
* Maintain clean entity separation and audit\-ready books

**Inventory \& Manufacturing Accounting Oversight**

* Review inventory accounting, COGS, and margin calculations
* Ensure accurate tracking of raw materials, WIP, and finished goods
* Validate inventory adjustments and variance explanations
* Support cost accounting methodology consistency

**Compliance, Controls \& Audit Support**

* Oversee sales tax, excise tax, and multi\-state compliance processes
* Coordinate with external CPA firm on tax filings and year\-end close
* Ensure lender reporting accuracy and timeliness
* Strengthen internal controls and segregation of duties
* Maintain accounting policies and procedures documentation

**Systems, Cleanup \& Process Improvement**

* Identify and correct structural issues in QuickBooks and related systems
* Guide historical cleanup projects where necessary
* Establish scalable accounting workflows and SOPs
* Reduce recurring close issues and rework

Pay: $30\.00 \- $50\.00 per hour

Work Location: In person

title: Strategic Financial Lead (Fractional or Retired CFO)
description:
**Benefits:**
* 401(k)
* Bonus based on performance
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance
**The Opportunity: Impact Without the Burnout**  

You’ve spent your career navigating complex financial waters, building enterprises, and mastering the art of the P\&L. Now, you’re looking for a way to keep your mind sharp and your expertise valued—without the daily corporate grind.  

We are a growing, EOS\-driven home care organization dedicated to providing exceptional care in our community. We don’t need a bookkeeper; we need a **Strategic Financial Leader**. We are seeking someone who enjoys the \\"puzzle\\" of gross margins and the discipline of forecasting to join our leadership team as a trusted advisor to our President.  

  

**Location:** Remote / Hybrid (Your Choice) **Commitment:** Part\-Time / Flexible Hours (10 Weekly)  

**Why This Role is Different**
* **True Autonomy:** You set your schedule. We value results and wisdom.
* **Mission\-Driven Work:** Every percentage point you save in margin erosion directly supports our ability to provide better care for our clients and caregivers.
* **Strategic Seat:** You will own the financial modeling and long\-range planning that dictates our growth, serving as a key member of our Leadership Team.
* **No \\"Tactical\\" Noise:** Your goal is to remove the President from the day\-to\-day tactical finance so they can focus on the vision.

**What You’ll Do**
* **Guard the Margin:** Monitor caregiver pay\-to\-bill ratios and identify margin leakage before it becomes a problem.
* **Forecast the Future:** Maintain a rolling 6\-12 month cash forecast and model the financial impact of hiring and expansion.
* **Lead the Scorecard:** Present financial clarity during our weekly Level 10 meetings, identifying and solving issues without defensiveness.
* **Ensure Discipline:** Oversee budget\-to\-actual variance and ensure our financial systems are scalable and audit\-ready.
* **Advise \& Educate:** Elevate the financial literacy of our leadership team, translating operational decisions into clear financial impacts.

**The Ideal Profile**
* **Seasoned Expertise:** You have a deep background in GAAP and enterprise\-level financial modeling.
* **The \\"CFO Mindset\\":** You are a data\-first decision\-maker who takes ownership.
* **EOS Familiarity:** Experience with the Entrepreneurial Operating System is a plus, while a disciplined, structured thinking style is a must.
* **High Ethics:** You possess a strong ethical compass and a direct, drama\-free communication style.
* **Systems Architect:** You find satisfaction in building scalable financial systems and documenting SOPs that allow the organization to grow without overwhelm.
* **The Gift of Clarity:** You can take complex GAAP concepts and \\"level them up\\" so the rest of the leadership team gains a higher level of financial literacy.

**Compensation \& Perks**
* **Competitive Part\-Time Compensation**
* **Performance Bonuses:** Tied to Gross Margin, Net Profit, and DSO targets.
* **Flexibility 10 Hours Weekly:** Control over your schedule—perfect for the professional seeking a rewarding \\"second act.\\"


Flexible work from home options available.

title: Senior Manager of Brand & Go-To-Market
description:
**company**  

“*A perfume is a world you can return to over and over again – a keyhole into far off places – material and imaginary*.”  

  

D.S. \& Durga is a Brooklyn fragrance house on a quest to create thrilling perfumes inspired by music, myth, and the mundane. Founded in 2009 by David (D.S.) and Kavi (Durga) Moltz, the brand is known for its uniquely distinguished candles, perfumes, and aromatic products.  

Now available in 600 doors across 40 countries, with five freestanding stores in NYC and LA, the brand is entering a new phase of growth following Manzanita Capital’s majority investment in 2024\. With a distinctive, cult\-like following and a fiercely original creative approach, D.S. \& Durga is uniquely positioned to scale its reach while preserving its artistic integrity.  

  

**role overview**  

The Senior Manager, Brand \& Go\-To\-Market is responsible for building and owning the marketing operating system at D.S. \& Durga.  

This is not a role stepping into an established structure. It is a role that helps define it.  

Working closely with the Fractional CMO, this individual will translate creative vision and product innovation into disciplined go\-to\-market execution — establishing the processes, tools, and rhythms required to scale the brand across retail, wholesale, and digital.  

This role sits at the intersection of strategy and execution. It requires someone who can move fluidly between defining how marketing should work and delivering against it day to day — building launch calendars, structuring toolkits, aligning teams, and ensuring every initiative is delivered on time and in full.  

The right candidate will be energized by a blank page. They will bring clarity where there is ambiguity, structure where there is none, and elevate execution without compromising the creative integrity that defines D.S. \& Durga.  

  

**context for the role**  

D.S. \& Durga is in the process of building its marketing function.  

There is not yet a fully established team or playbook. This role will operate with a high degree of autonomy and responsibility, helping define priorities, establish processes, and identify future capability needs as the function evolves.  

This requires a profile that is comfortable operating without full structure — while building it.  

  

**key responsibilities**  

  

Global Brand Marketing \& Storytelling* Translate founder\-driven creative direction into structured briefs, messaging frameworks, and actionable narratives for launches and core franchises.
* Maintain and evolve brand consistency across all channels (Global, U.S., DTC, wholesale, retail, digital).
* Develop brand and campaign decks in partnership with Creative; ensure cross\-functional clarity on story, priorities, and execution.
* Support product positioning, claims, and storytelling with structure and clarity — without diluting the brand’s specificity.

  

Product Marketing \& Innovation Support* Partner with Product Development and Marketing leadership on product briefs, innovation roadmaps, and launch prioritization.
* Translate trends, consumer insights, and category dynamics into actionable product and positioning recommendations.
* Frame product stories with clarity: hero claims, reasons\-to\-believe, ingredient narratives, and experiential cues.
* Ensure packaging, sampling, and collateral support the overall product strategy and brand standards.

  

U.S. Go\-To\-Market \& Trade Marketing (Critical)* Own end\-to\-end U.S. GTM planning and execution across wholesale partners including Nordstrom, Neiman Marcus, Bluemercury, Holt Renfrew, and key specialty stores.
* Build retailer sell\-in decks, seasonal toolkits, and account\-specific activation plans.
* Define, build, and ensure delivery of launch toolkits (retail, digital, and marketing) in partnership with Creative and Digital teams.
* Manage retail marketing programs: animations, placements, merchandising guidelines, co\-op activities, and account\-specific initiatives.
* Oversee POSM planning (samples, testers, GWPs, collateral) in coordination with Operations and Sales.
* Lead retailer.com execution, including briefs for PDP copy, assets, and brand pages.

  

Launch Operations \& Calendar Ownership (Critical)* Own the integrated 12–18 month launch and brand calendar across all channels.
* Drive critical path planning, launch readiness checkpoints, and asset delivery tracking.
* Ensure all launches are delivered on time and in full, with complete assets, aligned messaging, and cross\-channel readiness.
* Manage briefing, routing, approvals, and documentation for each launch.
* Partner with Operations and Sales on timing, forecasts, and asset needs.

  

Cross\-Functional Integration* Serve as the central connector across Creative, Product Development, Sales, Retail, Digital, Social, Influencer, PR, and Operations.
* Ensure each team receives the right narratives, assets, priorities, and timelines for every launch.
* Lead cross\-functional launch meetings, including agendas, recaps, timelines, and deliverables.
* Partner closely with Social Media and Influencer teams to align storytelling and amplification with launch priorities.

  

Performance Tracking \& Commercial Insights* Track and analyze launch performance (sell\-through, velocity, rank, traffic, engagement).
* Lead post\-mortems and integrate learnings into future plans.
* Maintain competitive intelligence and category insights.
* Provide recommendations that influence sales, marketing strategy, and product decisions.

  

**professional qualifications*** 8–12 years of experience in beauty, fragrance, or luxury lifestyle.
* Demonstrated experience across brand marketing, launch operations, and trade marketing.
* Proven track record of owning end\-to\-end go\-to\-market processes.
* Strong experience working with prestige wholesale partners and retailer.com environments.
* Highly organized with strong project management and process\-building capabilities.
* Strong communication skills with ability to translate complexity into clarity.
* High level of aesthetic sensibility and appreciation for brand craft.

  

**personal characteristics*** Builder mindset: comfortable operating without structure and motivated to create it.
* Strong operator: disciplined, organized, and accountable for execution.
* Hybrid thinker: combines creativity, commercial awareness, and operational rigor.
* Cross\-functional leader: able to influence without formal authority.
* Calm under pressure; able to prioritize effectively between urgent and important.
* Curious, culturally aware, and deeply engaged with brand, product, and storytelling.

  

**hybrid role summary**  

This role blends three core marketing disciplines:* 30% Brand \+ Campaign Operations
* 30% Product Marketing \+ Innovation Support
* 40% GTM \+ Trade Marketing \+ Launch Execution

  

It forms the operational backbone of the marketing function and plays a central role in scaling D.S. \& Durga’s next phase of growth.  

  

**additional information**  

Location: Brooklyn, NY (Hybrid\- 3 days per week in office)  

Salary Banding: $125,000\-$140,000 depending on experience and skills.

* Brilliant team of the strongest A players from McKinsey, Nexters, Gett, Glovo.
* Great opportunities for growth.

title: Client Strategist
description:
**Who We Are**

Disruptors Media is an AI\-powered marketing agency based in North Salt Lake, Utah. We serve as fractional CMO and Chief AI Officer for businesses across multiple industries, specializing in AI automation, lead generation, content creation, and marketing systems that actually drive revenue.

Our core philosophy is simple: AI should free you from robotic tasks, allowing you to be more human. We don’t just talk about AI – we build the systems, run the campaigns, and deliver the results that transform how our clients operate and grow.

We’re a small, high\-output team that punches well above its weight. Every person here has a direct, measurable impact on our clients’ success and the company’s growth. There is no hiding, no coasting, and no bureaucracy. If you want to be a cog in a machine, this isn’t the place. If you want to build something that matters alongside people who care, keep reading.

**The Role**

We’re looking for a Client Strategist who will own the client relationship from onboarding through renewal and expansion. This is not a passive relationship management role where you check in once a month and forward support tickets. This is a strategic position where you become the most valuable person on your client’s vendor list.

You’ll be the person our clients trust to understand their business, anticipate their needs, solve their problems, and consistently deliver results that make them look brilliant to their leadership.

You’ll run weekly strategy calls, lead onboarding for new clients, identify growth opportunities, and be the connective tissue between our clients and every internal team at Disruptors Media.

The right person for this role isn’t just organized and friendly. They’re strategic thinkers who can see around corners, communicate with authority at every stakeholder level, and take genuine pride in making other people successful.

**What You’ll Do**

**Own the Client Relationship**

* Serve as the primary point of contact for a portfolio of clients, building deep, trusted advisor relationships
* Run weekly strategy calls that clients actually look forward to – not status updates, but conversations that move their business forward
* Lead quarterly business reviews that demonstrate ROI, surface risks, and map the path to bigger outcomes
* Become an expert in each client’s business: their industry, their competitors, their goals, and the internal dynamics that drive their decisions

**Drive Client Onboarding and Adoption**

* Lead onboarding calls that set the tone for the entire partnership – establishing success criteria, building the 90\-day plan, and creating mutual accountability
* Ensure clients achieve first value within the first 30 days and full adoption within 90 days
* Proactively identify and remove blockers that slow down implementation or adoption
* Train clients on our services, tools, and deliverables so they understand and can articulate the value they’re receiving

**Retain and Expand Accounts**

* Monitor client health through engagement signals, deliverable quality, communication patterns, and satisfaction indicators
* Identify upsell and cross\-sell opportunities by understanding evolving client needs and matching them to our service offerings
* Lead renewal conversations with confidence, backed by data\-driven ROI analysis and clear forward\-looking strategy
* When a client is at risk, take ownership of the recovery: diagnose the problem, build the plan, execute the save

**Communicate Proactively and Strategically**

* Deliver proactive updates, insights, and recommendations before the client has to ask
* Frame every conversation – good news, bad news, pricing, and recommendations – with intention and strategic clarity
* Send flawless follow\-up recaps within two hours of every client meeting with clear action items, owners, and deadlines
* Coordinate internally across our content, automation, video, and engagement teams to ensure seamless delivery

**Contribute to Agency Growth**

* Turn happy clients into advocates who generate referrals, testimonials, and case studies
* Provide feedback from the field that improves our services, processes, and client experience
* Help build and refine the playbooks, templates, and systems that scale our client success operations

**Who You Are**

**The Non\-Negotiables**

* You live in or are willing to relocate to the greater Salt Lake area. This is an in\-person role at our North Salt Lake office. No exceptions.
* You have 2\+ years of experience in account management, customer success, client services, sales, or a similar client\-facing role.
* You are an exceptional communicator – in writing, on video calls, and in person. You can talk to a marketing coordinator and a CEO in the same afternoon and adjust your approach seamlessly.
* You are organized to the point of obsession. Multiple accounts, multiple timelines, multiple stakeholders – nothing falls through your cracks.
* You take ownership. When something goes wrong, you don’t point fingers. You fix it, learn from it, and make sure it doesn’t happen again.
* You are genuinely curious about how businesses work. You ask questions because you want to understand, not just because it’s on a script.

**What Sets You Apart**

* Experience in a marketing agency, SaaS company, or professional services environment
* Familiarity with AI tools, marketing automation platforms, or CRM systems like GoHighLevel, HubSpot, or Salesforce
* A track record of retaining and expanding client accounts – not just maintaining them
* Comfort with data: you can pull usage metrics, build a simple ROI analysis, and present numbers in a way that drives decisions
* Experience leading business reviews, onboarding calls, or strategic planning sessions with clients
* You’ve read a book on customer success, negotiation, or consultative selling in the last year because you actually wanted to, not because someone told you to

**What a Typical Week Looks Like**

We don’t want you guessing what this job actually feels like day to day. Here’s a realistic snapshot of a typical week as our Client Strategist.

**Monday**  \- Weekly team meeting/capacitation. Review portfolio health and prep for the week. Internal team sync to align on priorities and client deliverables. 2–3 client strategy calls. Send follow\-up recaps with action items within two hours of each call.

**Tuesday** \- Prep for client calls: pull data, draft agendas, identify insights. Send pre\-call agendas to clients. 2–3 client strategy calls. Coordinate with content and automation teams on deliverable status.

**Wednesday** \- Deep work: build QBR presentations, onboarding plans, or ROI analyses for upcoming conversations. New client onboarding call. Proactive outreach to accounts that haven’t had a touchpoint this week.

**Thursday** \- 2–3 client strategy calls. Follow\-up on open action items from earlier in the week. Assign VA’s to complete various tasks. Internal debrief on at\-risk accounts. Expansion pipeline review with leadership.

**Friday** \- Portfolio review: update CRM, log health scores, flag risks. Prepare any client materials for next week. Wrap up loose ends. Send proactive weekend updates to any clients with active projects. Plan next week.

**What We Offer \- Benefit Details**

Competitive Pay $3,000 – $5,000 per month based on experience, with a clear performance\-based growth trajectory. Client strategists would gain a percentage of their overall monthly recurring revenue that they personally manage.

Growth Potential You’re joining a fast\-growing agency at the ground floor. The people who join now and perform will lead teams later. Your pay is proportionate to the value you bring to the company.

AI Fluency You’ll work with cutting\-edge AI tools daily. The skills you build here are the most in\-demand capabilities in the market right now.

Real Impact No bureaucracy, no red tape. Your ideas get heard, your work gets seen, and your impact is measured in real client outcomes.

Training Playbooks \& Comprehensive onboarding with proven frameworks for every client scenario: onboarding, strategy calls, upselling, framing, difficult conversations, and more.

Team Culture A tight\-knit, ambitious team that works hard, supports each other, and is building something we’re all proud of.

Skill Acquisition The client strategist will quickly learn incredibly valuable skills, like how to harness AI effectively, as well as vital human\-skills (sales, effective communication, networking, etc.).

**This Role Is Not for You If…**

We believe in transparency. This role isn’t for everyone, and that’s okay. Save yourself the time if any of these apply:

* You prefer working from home. This is an in\-person position and that’s not changing.
* You see client management as checking boxes and forwarding emails. We need strategic thinkers, not task runners.
* You avoid difficult conversations. This role requires you to deliver tough news, push back on clients when needed, and hold people accountable – including yourself.
* You wait to be told what to do. We need someone who sees what needs to happen and makes it happen without being asked.
* You’re uncomfortable with AI tools and don’t want to learn them. AI is core to everything we do.
* You want a predictable, low\-pressure 9\-to\-5\. This is a startup environment. Some weeks are intense. The payoff is disproportionate growth.

**How to Apply**

If you’ve read this far and you’re thinking “this is exactly what I’ve been looking for,” here’s what we want from you:

Step 1: Submit your application through Indeed with your resume.

Step 2: In your cover letter or message, answer this one question: Tell us about a time you turned a struggling client relationship into one of your best accounts. What did you do, and what was the result? If you don’t have a client example, tell us about any relationship you repaired through intentional effort.

Step 3: If we like what we see, we’ll schedule a conversation. No five\-round interview gauntlet.

We move fast.

Disruptors Media is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate candidates based on their ability, their energy, and their alignment with our mission – nothing else.

**Ready to disrupt? Apply now.**

Pay: $3,000\.00 \- $5,000\.00 per month

Work Location: In person

title: Engagement and Account Growth Manager
description:
**Job Role:** Engagement \& Growth Account Manager


**Job Type**\- Full\-Time


**Location**\- Dallas,TX (Onsite)


**Profile Summary:**



We are seeking a **U.S. based Account Growth Manager** to drive **in\-person account growth and sustained growth** within mid\-market customers. This role focuses on **consultative engagement, executive relationship building, strategic networking, and revenue expansion** — not traditional staffing or purely client maintenance.


You will operate as the **Primary Account manager** between our U.S. customers and India delivery teams, confidently engaging CIOs, CTOs, Procurement leaders, and technical stakeholders. Deep technical leadership is *not* required, but the ability to understand customer technology needs and translate them into business outcomes is essential.


**What You’ll Do**


**Account Growth**


* Drive **in\-person account growth** across assigned mid\-market accounts.
* Identify and create **new revenue opportunities** within existing customers.
* Build multi\-threaded executive relationships (Procurement, CIO/CTO, IT, business leaders).
* Lead consultative sales engagements from opportunity discovery through close.


**Executive \& Technical Engagement**


* Communicate credibly with senior technical and business stakeholders.
* Understand customer challenges and position tailored solution conversations.
* Partner with fractional technical experts when deeper solution insights are required.


**Strategic Networking**


* Attend and represent the company at **industry, procurement, and CIO/CTO events**.
* Leverage personal and professional networks to advance opportunities.
* Establish visibility across target accounts and segments.


**Customer\-India Interface**


* Operate independently as the **U.S. commercial lead** without dependency on leadership handholding.
* Translate customer needs into actionable scopes for offshore delivery teams.


**Who You Are**


**Must\-Have**


* 5\+ years in **consultative sales, complex solutions selling, engagement management, or strategic account roles**.
* Proven ability to **drive revenue growth** and mid\-market accounts.
* Comfortable engaging C\-suite, especially **CTO/CIO and Procurement leaders**.
* Strong networking experience at industry forums or executive events.
* Ability to self\-lead, prioritize, and operate independently within a U.S. market context.
* Full\-time commitment (no concurrent external consulting).


**Preferred**


* Background in **consulting or solution selling** (preferred over pure staffing experience).
* Experience in **technology, digital transformation, or AI solution engagements**.
* Prior success engaging technical leaders and aligning business\-focused outcomes to technical needs.


**Not a Fit**


* Pure staffing/recruiter sales backgrounds without consultative complexity.
* Account roles limited to administrative servicing or retention\-only functions.
* Requires on\-the\-job training to engage executive or technical audiences.


**Why This Role Matters**



This is a **growth\-first, in\-person, strategic role** designed to expand our presence in the U.S. mid\-market. You will be entrusted with driving real revenue impact, shaping customer perception, and strengthening executive brand relationships through proactive engagement and strategic influence.


**Why Join Us?**



**Work directly with visionary leadership** in a fast\-moving, high\-growth environment.  

**Be at the center of client relationships** and play a key role in long\-term success.  

**Make an impact by driving revenue, retention, and community growth.**  

**Expand your professional network through high\-value industry events and strategic partnerships.**


**ABOUT ISHIR**



ISHIR is a digital innovation and enterprise AI services provider. We work with startups and enterprises to shape the future through accelerated innovation, deep technical expertise, access to global digital talent and a passion for complex problem\-solving. With our help, our clients overcome their most difficult digital challenges leveraging AI.



We are not just consultants, we are partners in our clients’ success, assisting them with re(gaining) competitive edge by identifying opportunities for differentiation, industry disruption, scalable innovation, and go\-to\-market strategies that deliver successful outcomes.



At ISHIR, we help bold businesses accelerate innovation through Talent, Speed\-to\-Market, and AI. We help make an impact by solving real problems using innovation, improved customer experiences and the right technologies.



As an ISHIR employee, you will get the advanced training you need to be successful, and the opportunity to apply it. You must be passionate about technology, crave responsibility, and be eager to apply your knowledge to real business solutions for our startup and enterprise customers. These are the qualities of a person destined for success at ISHIR.



ISHIR attracts a special type of individual—someone who is proactive, thrives on challenges, feeds off success, and looks at moving targets not as obstacles but as opportunities. ISHIR is an exciting place to work. It is imbued with an entrepreneurial spirit and promotes self\-reliance, open communication, and collaboration.


**Ready to Elevate Your Career? Apply Today!**

title: Content and Social Media Specialist
description:
**Content \& Social Media Specialist**  
**Type:** FTE (40 hrs/week)  
**Schedule:** 8 AM–5 PM CT (First 3 months in\-office 5 days per week. After 3 months is a hybrid schedule; 3 days in\-office, 2 days WFH.)

**About Expert Computer Solutions (ECS)**

ECS is a Houston\-based IT managed service provider helping small and mid\-sized businesses with outsourced IT, cybersecurity, and fractional CTO services. We serve industries including healthcare, legal, and manufacturing, helping companies stay secure, compliant, and productive.

**Role Overview**

As our Content \& Social Media Specialist, you’ll own high\-volume content creation and social media for ECS. You'll be the person who makes ECS look great: writing, designing, filming, and posting content that positions us as the go\-to IT partner for growing Houston businesses.

**This is a creative production role.** You'll be writing blog posts, designing social graphics, editing video clips, and managing our social media presence, using AI tools like ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot to work faster and smarter.

**Key Responsibilities**

* **Blog \& Website Content:** Research and write thought\-leadership posts on IT, cybersecurity, and technology trends and services. Create landing page content for vertical\-specific campaigns.
* **On\-Page SEO:**Optimize website content for search engines and AI search. Update meta titles, headings, and image alt text based on keyword briefs to improve search rankings.
* **Social Media Management \& Content Creation:** Own all social media channels: LinkedIn, Google Business Profile, Facebook, Instagram, Reddit. Create and manage the content calendar (blog, social, video). Post and comment consistently, and design social graphics, brand materials, presentations, and visual content in Canva.
* **Ad Copywriting:** Write ad copy for Google Ads, LinkedIn campaigns and other advertising channels**.**
* **Sales \& Marketing Collateral Production:** Handle internal brand requests. Develop case studies, one\-pagers, and sales collateral using Canva and AI tools. Produce promotional materials for webinars and events. Create and format postcards and mailer copy/design mockups.
* **Data Exports \& Trend\-Flagging:** Pull weekly performance metrics. Track and report on content performance: engagement rates, blog traffic, SEO rankings, follower growth.
* **Collaboration \& Meetings:** Participate in weekly team syncs and 1\-on\-1s.

**Qualifications**

*Must\-haves:*

* 2\-3 years of marketing experience (agency or in\-house)
* Strong writing skills — you can take a technical topic and make it accessible
* Design skills — you're comfortable in Canva and can make things look polished and professional
* Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, Copilot, or similar) — you see AI as a productivity multiplier, not a threat
* Social media management experience — you've managed business accounts (LinkedIn preferred)
* Basic understanding of SEO and content marketing
* Self\-starter who can own projects from brief to publish
* Houston\-based, able to be in\-office 3 days per week

*Nice\-to\-haves:*

* Video editing experience (Canva video, CapCut, or similar)
* Experience in B2B or technology marketing
* Graphic design beyond Canva (Adobe Creative Suite)
* Understanding of analytics tools (Google Analytics, social media insights)
* Familiarity with SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar)

**Why Work For ECS**

* Competitive salary with benefits
* Hybrid work schedule (3 days in\-office, 2 remote)
* AI tools provided (ChatGPT or Microsoft Copilot license, Canva Pro)
* Opportunity to shape the content and brand voice of a growing company
* Small team environment — your work will be visible and impactful

**Starting pay:** $20\.19/hr

· Submit your Cover Letter and tell us about your proudest real\-world marketing project

Note: Please don't apply if you're looking for a purely strategic role or if juggling multiple tasks stresses you out. We need someone who loves the details and can thrive in a support role while learning the ropes of marketing strategy.
