title: Head of Engineering (Ruby on Rails)
description:
We're hiring a **Head of Engineering!**


### **Summary**


* **Flexible Title:** Can tailor to reflect your skills \& experience.
* **Flexible Time:** Can do full\-time, part\-time, side\-gig (off\-hours), or fractional (contract).
* **Flexible Commitment:** Can do short\-term, long\-term, or intermittent.


**Why so flexible?** We're a FUNDED startup racing to launch end of Q1 2026\. That gives us just 3 months to stack features while raising additional working capital. Feel free to jump in, help us ship, then bounce \>\> or stick around. A successful launch translates into lots of permanent jobs for those that want them. We're also interested in long term "side gig" relationships, if that's what you're into \- in our experience, a few expert hours often beat full\-time learning\-curve hours.


### **About Us**


We're a credible, **funded**, remote\-first startup led by a serial \[technical] founder, and backed by a 20\-person team. The product is live in private alpha.


Learn more about our founder, team, and comp structures at **list\-lab.org**.


### **About The Role**


Can be a **purely management role** or a **hands\-on coding \+ management hybrid**. Either way, you must be deeply fluent in Rails and React — strong enough to spot\-check code quality and call BS on padded estimates or sandbagged velocity.


### **Compensation**


Up to **$400,000** max total compensation in Tier 1 cities; cash and equity components to be negotiated (amount reflects combined cash and equity components).


### **What Success Looks Like in 30 Days**


Many devs are currently using AI to achieve the same outcome with 70% less work. We want you to achieve the opposite: same effort, higher velocity.


* You've established yourself as the alpha nerd, and are running regular 1:1s with each developer
* You've established a positive culture that devs WANT to be part of
* You've established processes that ensure AI is being used to compound output
* You're holding the team accountable to the standard you've set


### **What You'll Do**


* Lead and manage a team of developers building on Ruby on Rails and React
* **Drive AI enablement across the organization** — integrate AI into the product, engineering workflows, and non\-technical processes to maximize output and efficiency
* Establish and track performance metrics for individual contributors and the team as a whole
* Implement systems for accountability, velocity tracking, and continuous improvement
* Be accountable for technical architecture decisions and code quality at scale (responsibility can be delegated to an architect or CTO)
* Conduct regular 1:1s, performance reviews, and sprint retrospectives with actionable outcomes
* Collaborate with product and design to translate business requirements into technical deliverables
* Coach developers and create training materials that raise the bar across the team
* Build and refine hiring, onboarding, and developer growth processes
* Identify and remove blockers — whether technical, process, or people\-related


### **What We're Looking For**


* **Deep technical expertise in Ruby on Rails, React**, and related stacks (PostgreSQL, Redis, Sidekiq, etc.) — strong enough to spot\-check PRs, validate estimates, and know when velocity numbers are bullshit
* **Hands\-on experience with AI enablement** — you've integrated AI tooling into products, dev workflows, and/or business processes
* Prior experience as a **Technical Manager, Engineering Director, VP of Engineering, or CTO**, at a venture\-backed company
* Demonstrated ability to drive developer performance through **metrics, not just vibes**
* Comfortable operating in a startup environment with ambiguity and rapid iteration
* Strong communicator who excels at expectation management
* Track record of building high\-performing engineering teams from the ground up

title: Cath Lab RN
description:
**Employment Type:**  

Part time
  
  

**Shift:**  

**Description:**  

Position is a .5
  
Registered Nurse – Cath Lab
  
  

**Department:** Heart Catheterization Lab
  
**Location:** Trinity Health Ann Arbor
  
About the Department
  
Heart Catheterization and Electrophysiology (EP) Lab
  
Our state\-of\-the\-art Heart Catheterization (Cath) and Electrophysiology (EP) Lab is dedicated to providing comprehensive cardiovascular care. We operate five advanced labs, including two dedicated EP labs, two Cath labs, and one versatile swing lab that can accommodate both types of procedures.
  
**Hybrid OR Capabilities:** Our facilities include a cutting\-edge Hybrid Operating Room (OR) that allows for seamless integration of both Cath and EP procedures, ensuring the highest level of patient care and procedural efficiency. Our hybrid cases focus on Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR), Watchman device implantation, Mitral Valve Repair (MVR), and device explants.
  
  

**Case Volume:**  

* Catheterization (Cath) Cases: We handle around 10\-14 Cath cases each day, focusing on diagnostic and interventional procedures such as coronary angiography, percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), stent placement, intravascular ultrasound (IVUS), fractional flow reserve (FFR) measurements, Chronic Total Occlusion (CTO) procedures, and treatment of STEMI (ST\-Elevation Myocardial Infarction).


Our team of highly skilled cardiologists, electrophysiologists, nurses, and technologists work collaboratively to deliver exceptional care, utilizing the latest technology and techniques to achieve the best possible outcomes for our patients.
  
  

**Shifts Available:** Varied shifts
  
  

REQUIRED EDUCATION AND LICENSURE
  
  

* Graduation from accredited nursing program.
* All new hires must have a BSN degree or must agree in writing to obtain their BSN within five (5\) years from date of hire or transfer to an RN role.
* Current Michigan Nursing License
* BLS


REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES
  
Knowledge of nursing theory, practice and age specific needs Interpersonal skills necessary to initiate and maintain collegial relationships with coworkers and therapeutic relationships with patients and families. Critical thinking skills necessary to perform principal duties and responsibilities of job description. Ability to concentrate and pay close attention to detail when planning and performing professional nursing care.
  
  

**Total Rewards and Benefits:**  

* Competitive compensation,DAILYPAY
* Benefits effective Day One! No waiting periods.
* Full benefits package including Medical, Dental, Vision, PTO, Life Insurance, Short and Long\-term Disability
* Retirement savings plan with employer match and contributions
* Opportunity for growth and advancement throughout SJMHS and Trinity Health
* Tuition Reimbursement


**What you will do:**  

* View patients holistically; considers physical, psychosocial, emotional, age specific, cultural and spiritual factors. Demonstrate appropriate assessment skills for identified patient populations.
* Demonstrate critical thinking skills by gathering and analyzing patient information, then responding to a patient situation.
* Develop, implement, evaluate and revise an individualized plan of care using evidenced based interventions and standards of care. Identify needs and begins to plan for care across the continuum considering patient and family preferences.
* Identify patient and family education needs. Develop and implement teaching plans based on evidence\-based practice, seeking out additional resources when necessary. Utilize teach back methodology to assure patient and family is learning effectively. Identify necessary resources to assure readiness for discharge and begins to develop plan in collaboration with other disciplines.
* Create an environment that promotes a positive patient experience.
* Communicate both verbal and written information (including documentation in the EMR) that is accurate, timely, concise and thorough. Effectively communicate patient status, priority goals and interventions to colleagues and other disciplines during consultation, rounds and handoffs.
* Maintains good rapport and cooperative relationships. Approaches conflict in a constructive manner. Helps to identify problems, offer solutions, and participate in their resolution.


Our Commitment
  
  

Rooted in our Mission and Core Values, we honor the dignity of every person and recognize the unique perspectives, experiences, and talents each colleague brings. By finding common ground and embracing our differences, we grow stronger together and deliver more compassionate, person\-centered care. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other status protected by federal, state, or local law.

title: Senior Python Backend Developer
description:
**Senior****Python****Backend****Developer**
============================================


Transform spreadsheet chaos into elegant data models that actually make sense. We need backend architects who can decode complex business processes and build systems that scale effortlessly.


Apply for the job
**We transform successful businesses by turning their proven processes into powerful software systems. We’re looking for developers who take pride in crafting solutions that help these businesses grow.**
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



The companies we work with are successful because they have people on their team who care deeply about what they do. They’ve grown for decades on talent, relationships, and sheer effort, often without the help of any modern technology. Eventually they hit a point where the business can’t scale any further on effort alone. That’s where we come in. We learn how their business actually works, then build the platform that turns their hard\-won processes into software that scales with them.



On our team, you’ll spend time improving and extending our platform across many client deployments. Django, complex data modeling, integrations with legacy systems. Every new client brings a new set of business rules and a fresh architecture challenge, so the work stays interesting without the churn of agency life (or the stress of startup life).


**The team**
------------



A small group of developers who care about the craft of building great software. Our CTO sets the bar for code quality across the platform. Our CEO is technical, has a history of designing and developing software, and serves as fractional CTO for our clients. Fully remote, async\-first. One monthly team meeting.


**What the job actually looks like**
------------------------------------


* Design data models that capture complex business rules (pricing tiers, approval workflows, multi\-step operational processes)
* Build and maintain APIs that connect modern and legacy systems
* Own projects end\-to\-end, from architecture through deployment
* Review and improve each other’s code (our CTO currently reviews all PRs and needs a peer who can share that platform responsibility)
* Ship new client projects on the platform regularly, each with its own domain complexity

**What we offer that most places don’t**
----------------------------------------


* **Protected development time.** No sprint ceremonies. Written updates over synchronous meetings.
* **New projects, one platform.** You get the variety of new client challenges without starting from scratch every time.
* **Direct access to leadership.** You’ll work with the CTO on architecture decisions, not through three layers of middle management.
* **An opinionated codebase.** We have established patterns and conventions. You’ll learn them, build within them, and help make them better over time.

**You’d be a good fit if**
--------------------------


* You have 8\+ years of Python backend experience (Django preferred, FastAPI/Flask acceptable)
* You’ve built and maintained production systems, not just features
* You use AI tools (Claude, Codex, etc.) as a natural part of your workflow
* You prefer writing over meetings
* You care about the details and obsess of code quality
* You can walk us through something you built and the decisions you made


**We're****not****just****cool****projects****and****good****looks**
--------------------------------------------------------------------


Our team is stacked with experts and our benefits are stacked with perks.


### **Health Insurance**


Company funded medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. Short and long term disability are offered as well.


### **Paid Time Off**


Paid time off is flexible and unlimited. You're encouraged to take time off for travel or just to get things done around the house.


### **Personal Leave**


Take extended time off when you need it whether it's health related or for new family additions.


### **Profit Sharing**


A portion of company profits are shared with employees each year.


### **Retirement Plan**


401K with Safe Harbor Matching Contribution up to 4% of your salary.


### **Perks**


Flexible start time and location, Summer Fridays, workspace allowance, and more.

title: Engagement and Account Growth Manager
description:
**Job Role:** Engagement \& Growth Account Manager


**Job Type**\- Full\-Time


**Location**\- Dallas,TX (Onsite)


**Profile Summary:**


We are seeking a **U.S. based Account Growth Manager** to drive **in\-person account growth and sustained growth** within mid\-market customers. This role focuses on **consultative engagement, executive relationship building, strategic networking, and revenue expansion** — not traditional staffing or purely client maintenance.


You will operate as the **Primary Account manager** between our U.S. customers and India delivery teams, confidently engaging CIOs, CTOs, Procurement leaders, and technical stakeholders. Deep technical leadership is *not* required, but the ability to understand customer technology needs and translate them into business outcomes is essential.


**What You'll Do**


**Account Growth**


* Drive **in\-person account growth** across assigned mid\-market accounts.
* Identify and create **new revenue opportunities** within existing customers.
* Build multi\-threaded executive relationships (Procurement, CIO/CTO, IT, business leaders).
* Lead consultative sales engagements from opportunity discovery through close.

**Executive \& Technical Engagement**


* Communicate credibly with senior technical and business stakeholders.
* Understand customer challenges and position tailored solution conversations.
* Partner with fractional technical experts when deeper solution insights are required.

**Strategic Networking**


* Attend and represent the company at **industry, procurement, and CIO/CTO events**.
* Leverage personal and professional networks to advance opportunities.
* Establish visibility across target accounts and segments.

**Customer\-India Interface**


* Operate independently as the **U.S. commercial lead** without dependency on leadership handholding.
* Translate customer needs into actionable scopes for offshore delivery teams.

**Who You Are**


**Must\-Have**


* 5\+ years in **consultative sales, complex solutions selling, engagement management, or strategic account roles**.
* Proven ability to **drive revenue growth** and mid\-market accounts.
* Comfortable engaging C\-suite, especially **CTO/CIO and Procurement leaders**.
* Strong networking experience at industry forums or executive events.
* Ability to self\-lead, prioritize, and operate independently within a U.S. market context.
* Full\-time commitment (no concurrent external consulting).

**Preferred**


* Background in **consulting or solution selling** (preferred over pure staffing experience).
* Experience in **technology, digital transformation, or AI solution engagements**.
* Prior success engaging technical leaders and aligning business\-focused outcomes to technical needs.

**Not a Fit**


* Pure staffing/recruiter sales backgrounds without consultative complexity.
* Account roles limited to administrative servicing or retention\-only functions.
* Requires on\-the\-job training to engage executive or technical audiences.

**Why This Role Matters**


This is a **growth\-first, in\-person, strategic role** designed to expand our presence in the U.S. mid\-market. You will be entrusted with driving real revenue impact, shaping customer perception, and strengthening executive brand relationships through proactive engagement and strategic influence.


**Why Join Us?**


**Work directly with visionary leadership** in a fast\-moving, high\-growth environment.  

**Be at the center of client relationships** and play a key role in long\-term success.  

**Make an impact by driving revenue, retention, and community growth.**  

**Expand your professional network through high\-value industry events and strategic partnerships.**


**ABOUT ISHIR**


ISHIR is a digital innovation and enterprise AI services provider. We work with startups and enterprises to shape the future through accelerated innovation, deep technical expertise, access to global digital talent and a passion for complex problem\-solving. With our help, our clients overcome their most difficult digital challenges leveraging AI.


We are not just consultants, we are partners in our clients' success, assisting them with re(gaining) competitive edge by identifying opportunities for differentiation, industry disruption, scalable innovation, and go\-to\-market strategies that deliver successful outcomes.


At ISHIR, we help bold businesses accelerate innovation through Talent, Speed\-to\-Market, and AI. We help make an impact by solving real problems using innovation, improved customer experiences and the right technologies.


As an ISHIR employee, you will get the advanced training you need to be successful, and the opportunity to apply it. You must be passionate about technology, crave responsibility, and be eager to apply your knowledge to real business solutions for our startup and enterprise customers. These are the qualities of a person destined for success at ISHIR.


ISHIR attracts a special type of individual—someone who is proactive, thrives on challenges, feeds off success, and looks at moving targets not as obstacles but as opportunities. ISHIR is an exciting place to work. It is imbued with an entrepreneurial spirit and promotes self\-reliance, open communication, and collaboration.


**Ready to Elevate Your Career? Apply Today!**

title: Software Consultant/Engineer (Full Stack .NET)
description:

title: Law Firm Administrator
description:
The Underwood Law Firm, P.C. is an award\-winning law firm that pioneered a growing real estate/ business niche with offices throughout California. In less than 5 years, the firm has grown from a solo practice to a 10 lawyer firm, and is growing again.

This is an executive\-level position for a professional legal administrator/ COO to oversee everything in the law firm, except the practice of law. Under the direction and supervision of the Firm's Owner/ President, this role has significant discretion and broad responsibility for the management of Firm's business operations and the supervision of non\-employees.

This includes not only strategic planning, project management, and critical administrative and financial oversight, but also a key leadership role in shaping and sustaining firm culture.

Specifically, the right candidate will be excellent at workflow and project management, deeply understand employee engagement, highly skilled at metrics and KPIs, adept at marketing campaigns, and who will be invested in making important contributions to the success of the Firm.

**Responsibilities**

The Administrator manages the operations and business functions of the Firm. The Administrator must also work closely with the Managing Attorney, CFO, Marketing Director and fractional/vendor advisors.

The Administrator is responsible to lead, manage, supervise, coach, and train the other managers as well as the non\-attorney staff, ensuring that they perform to expectations, including hiring/ firing, supervision, and discipline.

The Administrator is authorized to make purchases and commitments on behalf of the Firm to the extent permitted by the policies, procedures, and internal control documents.

The Administrator’s roles encompasses:

**(1\)** Leadership and Strategic Planning;

**(2\)** Administration of Key Business Systems;

**(3\)** Finance \& Accounting;

**(4\)** Human Resources Management;

**(5\)** Marketing and Sales;

**(6\)** Factory and Production;

**(7\)** Physical Plant.

**Qualifications**

The Administrator must have the equivalent of a bachelor’s degree in Business, Accounting or a related field, or equivalent work experience.

The Administrator will have significant supervisory experience and experience working in a professional services firm. Experience in law firm budgeting, bookkeeping, and human resources is highly preferred.

It is essential for the Administrator to be “in the know” about technology and profitability trends to ensure the Firm is maximizing technology and resource developments to increase capacity and efficiency.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $110,000\.00 \- $160,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Professional development assistance

Experience:

* legal management: 2 years (Required)

Work Location: Remote

title: Director of Finance
description:
Ghostworks is seeking a hands\-on Director of Finance to help build and scale the company’s financial infrastructure during a high\-growth phase.

  



This role will focus on establishing strong manufacturing finance processes, implementing cost and project accounting systems, building financial models and forecasts, and helping implement scalable financial systems.

  



Unlike a traditional CFO role, this position requires a **roll\-up\-your\-sleeves operator** who is comfortable working directly in spreadsheets and accounting systems while building the processes and tools needed to support rapid growth.

  



The Director of Finance will work closely with the COO, leadership team, and fractional CFO to prepare the organization for future scale and eventual transition to a full\-time CFO.

  


**Key Responsibilities**


**Manufacturing \& Project Accounting**


* Establish cost accounting and project/job costing systems appropriate for hybrid manufacturing and engineering projects
* Develop program\-level profitability tracking
* Implement labor, material, and overhead cost tracking methodologies
* Ensure accurate inventory, WIP, and cost allocation practices

  


**Financial Planning \& Analysis**


* Build and maintain financial models and forecasts
* Develop annual budgets and rolling forecasts
* Create pro\-forma financial projections for new programs and initiatives
* Provide leadership with clear financial performance metrics

  


**Systems \& Process Development**


* Evaluate and implement financial systems, including potential ERP platforms
* Improve financial data structure, chart of accounts, and reporting processes
* Integrate accounting processes with operational systems
* Establish scalable financial processes and internal controls

  


**Operational Finance \& Cash Management**


* Improve working capital management, including AR, AP, and inventory
* Support pricing, quoting, and margin analysis for new programs
* Partner with operations leadership to ensure financial discipline in project execution

  


**Strategic Finance Support**


* Support preparation of financial materials for lenders or potential investors
* Assist leadership with financial modeling and capital planning
* Work with fractional CFO on strategic financial initiatives

  


**Leadership \& Team Development**


* Partner with Accounting Manager to ensure strong financial operations
* Implement standard operating procedures for accounting and finance processes
* Help scale the finance function as the company grows

  


**Compliance**


* Assist with the reporting and tracking of areas like insurances, 1099s, etc
* Confirm documentation for employee expenses is up to date and follows company policies
* All other tax and year end reporting documentation

  


**Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or related field
* 10\+ years of progressive finance or accounting experience
* Strong experience in manufacturing finance, cost accounting, or project accounting
* Experience implementing financial systems or ERP platforms
* Strong financial modeling and Excel skills
* Experience building financial processes in growing organizations

  


**Preferred**:


* Experience in engineer\-to\-order or project\-based manufacturing
* Experience supporting capital raising or strategic planning
* CPA or MBA

  


**Key Attributes**



The ideal candidate will be:


* Highly hands\-on and detail oriented
* Comfortable building systems from scratch
* Operationally minded and pragmatic
* Able to translate financial data into operational insights
* Comfortable working in a lean, fast\-growing environment

title: Director of Operations - Fractional
company: Amber K. Boyd Attorney at Law
description:
AKB Law needs an experienced operator who can parachute in, create order from chaos, and build the systems that allow us to scale double our revenue.

  

This is a 6\-9 month engagement to:

* Install metrics and accountability systems
* Train the team to operate without constant founder involvement
* Identify and fix the biggest operational bottlenecks
* Potentially transition to full\-time Director of Operations (if that's a fit for both parties)

  

However, if both parties agree, this role can convert to full\-time Director of Operations at month 6\-9 with:

* Base salary: $75,000\-$95,000
* Performance bonus structure
* Full benefits package

**Compensation:**
$40 \- $55 hourly

**Responsibilities:****WHAT YOU'LL DO (20 Hours/Week)**

**Core Responsibilities:**

 **Weeks 1\-4: Assessment \& Quick Wins**

* Audit current operations, team structure, and metrics (or lack thereof)
* Interview the founder and team to identify bottlenecks
* Implement immediate improvements (low\-hanging fruit that creates time/clarity)
* Set up a basic revenue tracking dashboard
* Document current processes (even if they're broken)

**Deliverable:** Assessment report with top 5 priorities and 90\-day roadmap

 **Months 2\-3: Install Accountability Systems**

* Launch weekly metrics review with founder (Mondays, 30 min)
* Implement weekly leadership team meeting (you run it)
* Create a case tracking system (pipeline, velocity, settlement values)
* Build a hiring pipeline for the next key role (likely Senior Attorney)
* Train the team on new processes and hold them to it

**Deliverable:** Metrics dashboard live, weekly meetings running, at least 1 new SOP implemented

 **Months 4\-6: Build for Scale**

* Refine the case intake and qualification process
* Improve settlement velocity (identify delays, create playbooks)
* Hire and onboard at least 1 new team member
* Document all core processes (intake case management settlement close)
* Prepare for full\-time ops hire (could be you or someone else)

**Deliverable:** Firm operating with clear metrics, documented processes, founder spending \<30% time on case work

 **Months 7\-9: Transition**

* Either convert to a full\-time Director of Operations OR
* Hire and train your replacement (full\-time Director of Ops)
* Hand off all systems and processes
* Exit with firm operating independently

**Deliverable:** Full handoff documentation, firm ready to scale to $3M\+

 **Your Weekly Schedule (20 Hours)**

**Mondays (3 hours):**

* Review metrics from the previous week
* 30\-minute check\-in with the founder
* Leadership team meeting (1 hour)
* Identify the top 3 priorities for the week

**Tuesdays\-Thursdays (4\-5 hours/day):**

* Project work (process documentation, system implementation, hiring)
* Team check\-ins and accountability conversations
* Problem\-solving and firefighting as needed

**Fridays (2 hours):**

* Week\-in\-review: what got done, what didn't, why
* Prep for next week

**Asynchronous:**

* Available via Slack/email for urgent issues (1\-2 hour response time during business hours)
* Monthly strategy session with founder (90 minutes)

**Qualifications:**
**Required:**

* 7\+ years operations/management experience (law firm or professional services strongly preferred)
* You've done this before—you know what good operations look like
* Comfortable working independently with minimal direction
* Strong project management and communication skills
* Experience building systems from scratch (not just maintaining existing ones)
* Can work remotely but travel to Indianapolis monthly (or as needed)

**Ideal Background:**

* Former law firm COO/Director of Ops who now does fractional work
* Legal operations consultant with employment law experience
* Business operations expert who's scaled multiple small businesses
* EOS Implementer or similar framework experience

**You're a Fit If:**

* You can diagnose problems fast and implement solutions faster
* You're comfortable being the "bad guy" when accountability is needed
* You don't need credit—you care about results
* You can context\-switch between strategic thinking and execution
* You've built operations playbooks and know what works (and what doesn't)

**About Company**  

Amber K. Boyd Attorney at Law is a boutique law firm that represents clients in employment and probate matters. Our team is dedicated to providing high\-quality legal services and achieving the best outcomes for clients. We foster a collaborative work environment where the skills and expertise of every team member are valued and nurtured.

title: Fractional Controller
description:
Job Title: Controller

Location: \[Insert Location]

Reports To: Managing Partner / Executive Committee

Firm Size: \~$15 Million Annual Revenue

Position Summary

The Controller is responsible for overseeing all financial operations of the firm, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and strategic financial insight. This role manages accounting, financial reporting, budgeting, cash flow, partner compensation support, and regulatory compliance, with a strong emphasis on law firm–specific requirements such as trust accounting and billing realization.

The Controller serves as a key advisor to firm leadership, helping drive profitability, efficiency, and informed decision\-making.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Management \& Reporting

* Oversee all accounting functions including general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, payroll, and financial close
* Prepare and deliver timely monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
* Develop and maintain financial dashboards and KPI reporting (e.g., realization rates, utilization, profitability by practice area)
* Ensure compliance with GAAP and applicable regulatory standards

Law Firm Accounting \& Billing Oversight

* Manage client billing processes, including timekeeping, pre\-bill review, and collections
* Monitor and report on key metrics such as:
* Billable hours and utilization
* Realization and collection rates
* Work\-in\-progress (WIP) and aged receivables
* Oversee trust (IOLTA) accounting to ensure strict compliance with legal and ethical requirements

Budgeting, Forecasting \& Analysis

* Lead annual budgeting process and ongoing financial forecasting
* Provide variance analysis and actionable insights to partners and practice leaders
* Support pricing strategies, fee arrangements, and profitability analysis

Cash Flow \& Treasury Management

* Monitor and manage firm cash flow, operating accounts, and partner distributions
* Optimize working capital, including receivables and payables management
* Coordinate with banking partners and manage credit facilities if applicable

Partner Compensation \& Firm Economics

* Support development and administration of partner compensation models
* Provide analysis on partner performance, originations, and profitability
* Ensure transparency and accuracy in compensation reporting

Compliance \& Risk Management

* Ensure compliance with legal, tax, and regulatory requirements
* Coordinate with external auditors, tax advisors, and consultants
* Maintain internal controls and financial policies to safeguard firm assets

Leadership \& Team Management

* Lead and develop accounting and finance staff
* Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and service excellence
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with HR, IT, and practice groups

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

* Billing realization and collection rates
* Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
* WIP and AR aging
* Budget vs. actual performance
* Profitability by practice area and partner
* Cash flow predictability

Qualifications

Education \& Experience

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA strongly preferred)
* 7–12\+ years of progressive accounting/finance experience
* Prior experience in a law firm or professional services firm highly preferred
* Experience with legal billing systems and trust accounting required

Skills \& Competencies

* Strong understanding of law firm economics and operations
* Advanced financial analysis and modeling skills
* High attention to detail and accuracy
* Ability to communicate financial insights to non\-financial stakeholders
* Proficiency in accounting and practice management software (e.g., Elite, Aderant, Clio, QuickBooks)
* Leadership and team development capabilities

Personal Attributes

* High integrity and professionalism
* Proactive, solutions\-oriented mindset
* Ability to operate both strategically and tactically
* Strong organizational and time management skills
* Comfortable working in a partner\-driven environment

Pay: $62\.00 \- $75\.00 per hour

Work Location: In person

title: Accounting Manager - Client Lead
description:
**Graphite Financial** is a growing boutique finance, accounting, tax, and strategy firm (www.graphitefinancial.com) built to help startup and high\-growth companies scale efficiently and sustainably. Graphite Financial is redefining the “finance department” for early\-stage companies and VC\-backed startups. We deliver high\-caliber accounting and finance leadership and support to entrepreneurs and management teams building scalable, high\-growth companies. Our clients are actively managing cash burn, measuring performance, reporting to investors, making capital allocation decisions, and preparing for fundraising. All of that takes expertise and time that founders don’t have and can’t afford to hire for. Graphite bridges the gap with flexible, fixed\-fee support packages that provide fractional leadership and operational support from CFOs, Controllers, FP\&A Managers, and Tax leaders.

  

Graphite is looking for an experienced and process\-driven Accounting Manager/Client Lead ready to step into a high\-impact leadership role, guiding the financial strategy for a portfolio of high\-growth startup clients. We are seeking a dynamic and strategic leader with a proven record of establishing strong financial controls and successfully scaling accounting operations. This is a hands\-on position that requires deep technical expertise, exceptional client relationship skills, and a passion for the fast\-paced world of startups.

 **What You'll Be Leading**

  

This role is the direct owner of the client relationship, often partnering directly with the CEOs and executive teams of innovative, rapidly scaling companies. You won't just oversee, you'll strategize, implement, and deliver the financial clarity essential for their growth.

* **Client Leadership**: Serve as the primary, high\-touch contact for clients, owning the relationship and providing strategic advice on high\-level accounting, managerial accounting, and financial activities.
* **Team Development**: Manage, coach, and mentor a team of Staff and Senior Accountants, providing detailed review of their work to ensure impeccable quality and accuracy in financial reporting.
* **Operational Excellence**: Oversee all accounting functions, including the end\-to\-end monthly close process, financial statement preparation, and ensuring the smooth execution of day\-to\-day transactional work.
* **Strategic Project Management**: Coordinate and lead all activities for critical ad hoc projects, including external audits, due diligence support, and complex technical accounting research.
* **Financial Insight**: Deliver accurate, timely, and actionable client financial reporting to drive key decision\-making. This includes sophisticated analysis of key financial metrics, trends, and variances.
* **Cross\-Functional Collaboration**: Partner closely with our internal Finance and Tax departments to ensure every client's financial need is met with an exceptional level of customer service.

**Who You Are**

  

You are a strategic operator with the drive to build robust financial infrastructure for the next generation of industry leaders across a diverse portfolio of startups.

* **Experience:** You bring approximately 8\+ years of relevant client facing accounting experience (public accounting or corporate accounting), with at least 2 years of experience managing a team.
* **Education**: A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting is required. A CPA or MBA is highly preferred, but not mandatory.
* **Technical Expertise**: Expertise in Web3 (Crypto) accounting and/or Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) accounting is a plus.
* **System Proficiency:** Significant experience with core GL platforms like QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, or Sage Intacct, plus exposure to modern, tech\-forward systems (e.g., Bill, Ramp, Stripe, Gusto, Rippling, Cin7, Brex).
* **Communication**: Possess strong communication skills with the rare ability to translate complex accounting concepts into clear, concise language for clients, executives, and non\-financial stakeholders.
* **Mindset:** You are a team player comfortable in a fast\-paced environment, possessing a high degree of professionalism, motivation, and a genuine passion for working with growing startup companies

**Comprehensive Benefits \& Perks:**

* **Health \& Wellness:** Competitive Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage. 100% of the employee's health insurance premium is covered by the company.
* **Financial Security:** Company\-paid Short\-Term and Long\-Term Disability insurance.
* **Work/Life Balance To Include:** Generous Unlimited Paid Time Off (PTO), 11 Paid Company Holidays, and comprehensive Paid Parental Leave.
* **Remote Work Setup:** Fully remote position with company\-provided technology equipment and a $250 technology stipend to support your ideal home office setup.
* **401k:** With a company match


Compensation for this role is highly competitive and will be determined based on the successful applicant's experience with special consideration given to proven **Web3 (Crypto) and CPG accounting expertise** as well as geographic location.

 **The pay range for this role is:**  

96,050 \- 113,000 USD per year(Remote (United States))

title: Fractional CIO
description:
DDI is seeking a **Fractional CIO** to provide strategic and operational leadership across our IT and business systems landscape. This role will guide technology direction, optimize current systems, and ensure alignment with company growth and operational needs.


This is a **hands\-on strategic role** ideal for a seasoned IT leader who can balance vision with execution in a mid\-sized, fast\-moving organization.




**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop and execute DDI’s IT strategy aligned with business objectives
* Provide leadership and oversight of IT operations, infrastructure, and cybersecurity
* Optimize and integrate core business systems (e.g., HRIS, ERP, PLM)
* Partner with executive leadership (HR, Finance, Engineering, Operations) to support key initiatives
* Lead vendor strategy and performance management (e.g., managed service providers like Exceedio)
* Establish scalable processes, governance, and documentation across IT
* Assess and improve data integrity, reporting capabilities, and system integrations
* Support ongoing digital transformation initiatives
* Ensure compliance with security standards and industry best practices


Provide guidance on budgeting, cost optimization, and IT investments

**Qualifications**

* 15\+ years of progressive IT leadership experience, including CIO, VP IT, or equivalent roles
* Experience in manufacturing, semiconductor, or industrial environments strongly preferred
* Proven track record of leading IT strategy and system implementations in mid\-sized organizations
* Strong knowledge of:
	+ ERP systems (e.g., SYSPRO or similar)
	+ PLM systems (e.g., Arena or similar)
	+ IT infrastructure and cybersecurity frameworks
* Experience managing third\-party IT vendors and managed service providers
* Ability to operate both strategically and hands\-on




**Engagement Model**

* Fractional / part\-time (e.g., 1–3 days per week or as needed)
* Hybrid with periodic onsite presence in Scotts Valley
* Flexible structure based on business needs




**Success Factors**

* Quickly assess current IT landscape and identify gaps/opportunities
* Establish clear IT roadmap and priorities within first 60–90 days
* Improve system integration, reliability, and user experience
* Strengthen vendor accountability and performance
* Enable scalable infrastructure to support company growth

title: Fractional CISO Executive
description:
Apogee Global RMS is partnering with a rapidly scaling organization seeking an experienced **Fractional CISO** **Executive** to elevate their security posture, strengthen governance, and guide the company through its next phase of growth. The ideal leader brings strategic clarity, operational maturity, and the ability to build a security foundation that scales.

**Role Overview**


The Fractional CISO will serve as the organization’s senior‑most security executive, responsible for defining, maturing, and executing the enterprise security strategy. This role is suited for a seasoned cybersecurity leader who can operate at both strategic and hands‑on levels, advising executives while building practical, right‑sized controls.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Conduct a comprehensive assessment of the current security posture and identify critical gaps
* Develop and implement a scalable, long‑term security roadmap aligned with business growth
* Establish and mature governance, risk, and compliance frameworks (NIST, ISO, SOC2, etc.)
* Oversee incident response readiness, threat monitoring, and escalation protocols
* Advise executive leadership on risk prioritization, security investments, and tooling
* Partner with engineering, IT, HR, and operations to embed a security‑first culture
* Provide clear, executive‑level reporting on risks, controls, and strategic recommendations
* Support audit readiness and regulatory compliance initiatives as needed

  

**Requirements**  

* Proven 8\-10 plus years of experience as a CISO, vCISO, or senior cybersecurity executive
* Demonstrated success building and maturing security programs in scaling organizations
* Deep expertise in GRC, risk management, and security architecture
* Strong ability to anticipate threats and implement proactive controls
* Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate technical risk into business impact
* Experience advising executive teams and influencing cross‑functional decision‑making

 **Benefits** **Why Choose Apogee:**

* Fractional executive role with flexible hours
* High‑impact opportunity to architect and mature the client’s security foundation
* Long‑term engagement potential based on performance and organizational needs

**How to Apply**


For any questions (OR) to apply, please contact us at **careers@apogeeglobalrms.com**.

title: Fractional Safety Director
description:
Job Title: Fractional Safety Director (Remote)

Employment Type: Fractional / Contract Location: Remote (U.S.\-based) Travel Requirement: Up to 25%

Company Overview

Fortify Risk Management is a dynamic, growth company, focused on delivering Fractional Risk Management and Employee Safety Consulting in a fee\-for\-service platform. We do this in a fun, interactive, and cutting\-edge way. We are taking a normally boring topic, and adventur\-izing it, driving real risk reduction results. www.fortifyriskmgmt.com

Position Overview

We are seeking an experienced Fractional Safety Director to work with our team of industry pros. You will provide strategic and operational safety leadership in a remote capacity. This role is ideal for a senior safety professional with 5\+ years of experience who can design, implement, and oversee comprehensive safety programs while working remotely and traveling periodically to support field operations.

The Fractional Safety Director will serve as a key advisor to our client companies’ executive leadership. This individual will drive regulatory compliance, risk mitigation, and continuous improvement across clients.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Safety Leadership

· Implement company\-wide safety initiatives in coordination with our risk management team.

· Align safety initiatives with operational and business goals

· Educate and build relationships with key safety personnel inside our clients operations.

· Lead continuous improvement efforts across departments

Compliance \& Risk Management

· Advise on compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local regulations

· Oversee policy development and regulatory updates

· Direct incident investigations

· Monitor and manage workers’ compensation trends and claims

Program Development

· Design scalable safety management systems

· Develop standardized safety procedures and training programs

· Conduct virtual audits, supplemented with on\-site visits as needed

· Support emergency preparedness planning

Training \& Culture

· Deliver remote leadership coaching and safety training

· Promote a proactive and accountable safety culture

· Facilitate safety committee meetings and engagement initiatives

Qualifications

· Bachelor’s degree in Occupational Safety, Environmental Health \& Safety, or related field (or equivalent experience)

· 5\+ years of progressive safety leadership experience

· Experience supporting multi\-site operations

· Experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

· Professional certifications (CSP, CIH, CHST, or equivalent) preferred

· Strong regulatory knowledge and audit experience

· Excellent communication and executive presentation skills

· Ability and willingness to travel up to 25%

· Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a professional demeanor and business casual dress attire. Maintain strict confidentiality with all materials.

· Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail. Ability to take direction well \& work independently and in a team environment.

· Can\-do attitude, with an positive mindset. Tenacity on project completion. Kind and thoughtful in supporting our consultants. Innovative.

Compensation \& Structure

· Competitive hourly consulting. flexible fractional engagement, with additional material development and referral compensation, as well.

Pay: $40\.00 \- $60\.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

title: Executive Assistant
description:
About Us: We are a dynamic, growth company, focused on delivering Fractional Risk Management and Employee Safety Consulting, in a fee\-for\-service platform. We do this in a fun, interactive, and cutting\-edge way. We are taking a normally boring topic, and adventur\-izing it. We drive success is reducing our client’s risk and improving worksite safety.

Position Summary:

We’re currently looking for an Executive Assistant to be a supportive force who empowers our senior leadership. The ideal candidate will be a proactive problem\-solver with exceptional communication skills and meticulous attention for details. This person should have experience working in an office environment, performing administrative duties, providing support to managers, and potentially some social media editing skills.

Given the dynamic nature of the executive landscape, we rely on an administrative lead to be flexible and consistent while maintaining high standards of excellence and professionalism. This individual will strive to collaborate and communicate effectively with remote and in\-person team members. You will be working in an office and a part of a remote team environment. We invest in our teams and look to build each other up. Together, we bring excellence to life!

Key Responsibilities:

* Support the Principal Executive primarily and provide additional support to Risk Management consultants \& team members, as needed, to ensure that company goals and objectives are accomplished and that operations run efficiently.
* Build, maintain, and refine internal processes and expedite workflows.
* Provide administrative and office support (typing, Excel, PowerPoint, filing system and contacts database maintenance).
* Prepare client proposals and work product, with executive direction.
* Coordinate scheduling and calendar management.
* Review and edit professional content as support for consultants.
* Coordinate events like training seminars, webinars, and other client relations events.
* Excel in communication with clients, employees, and independent contractors.
* Manage office client/project timeline.
* Orchestrate senior executives’ travel logistics and activities, as needed.
* Other general tasks as requested, from time to time.

Required and Preferred Skills and Experience:

· Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a professional demeanor and business casual dress attire. Maintain strict confidentiality with all materials.

· Proficiency with Microsoft Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), LinkedIn, and YouTube postings. Canva is a plus.

· Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail. Ability to take direction well \& work independently.

· Timeline driven with a high excellence standard. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, with a fun and collaborative attitude.

· Can\-do attitude, with a positive mindset. Tenacity on project completion. Kind and thoughtful in supporting our consultants. Innovative.

· Organize team communications and meetings.

· Preferred three years of experience in administrative support, executive support, or office operations.

Compensation:

Compensation will be salary and will range from 60,000 to 75,000 per year, depending upon experience. Employee benefits may also be offered.

Environment:

We are a fun, fast\-paced, and tech\-enhanced consulting firm that elevates the game of those around us through quality interactive tools to manage corporate risk and safety.

How to Apply: Please submit your resume and explain why you are a good fit for this position.

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $75,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

title: Director of Residential Finance
description:
**Company Description**  

Auberge Collection is a portfolio of extraordinary hotels, resorts, residences and private clubs. While each property is unique, all share a crafted approach to luxury and bring the soul of the locale to life through captivating design, exceptional cuisine, innovative wellbeing, and gracious yet unobtrusive service. With 30 one\-of\-a\-kind hotels, resorts, and residences, Auberge invites guests to create unforgettable stories in some of the world’s most desirable destinations.


For more information: auberge.com


Connect with Auberge Collection on Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and LinkedIn @Auberge and \#AlwaysAuberge

 **Job Description**  

The Director of Residential Finance serves as a strategic leader for all financial aspects of the organization’s residential operations, ensuring effective financial planning, robust internal controls, and compliance across residential projects and properties. The Director plays a key role in leading financial decision\-making for residential initiatives, with a focus on accuracy, efficiency, and adherence to budgetary and regulatory requirements. This role has a stronger partnership and working relationship with the Director of Residential Operations in oversight and strategy related to Residential Operations.


Primary areas of responsibility and oversight include developing and implementing an effective control environment, coordination of local compliance submissions, oversight of third\-party transactional service partners, support of internal controls and audits, financial reviews for residential properties, strategic ownership of the annual budget planning, policy and procedure development for HOAs and all residential rental and finance programs, guidance and collaborates on technology solutions, property team training, and talent acquisition support and pre\-opening planning and residential critical path.


Key Responsibilities


Residential Financial Management Strategy

* Collaborate with operations and finance leaders to develop and effectively implement residential financial management strategies and processes.
* Ensure Auberge’s residential financial reporting conforms to the hotels’ respective accounting standards (i.e., US GAAP, IFRS, or other local standards) and management agreements.
* Develop and implement effective residential policies and procedures to maintain a strong internal control environment.
* Research, evaluate, recommend, and lead the implementation of residential technology solutions to create efficient and effective transaction processing and information sharing.
* Collaborate with Internal Audit and Operations Finance leadership to establish and regularly update audit and financial review checklists.
* Participate in annual continuing professional education on residential accounting matters and advise Auberge senior management on emerging industry accounting issues and best practices.


Third\-Party Consultants \& Services Management

* Develop a scope of services for potential third\-party accounting service providers, and lead the bidding and evaluation process for contract negotiations.
* Oversee and manage relationships with third\-party transactional and accounting service providers.
* Review negotiations of terms and contracts with third\-party providers.
* Collaborate with the hotel finance team to adapt Auberge’s residential accounting policies and procedures into the third\-party accounting provider’s services. Evaluate and document third party’s departures from Auberge’s policies and procedures. Develop SOPs and workflows integrating third\-party services with internal processes.
* Provide support to property finance teams to resolve transactional issues.


Internal Control \& Audit

* Coordinate with property finance teams and the Director of Internal Audit on all audit activities.
* Review audit scope, reports, management letter, findings, and observations.
* Coordinate with teams on remediation plans and provide support.
* Continuously improve internal control processes for residential finance.


Residential Financial Management \& Compliance

* Partner with the Director of Internal Audit to develop and update control standards related to residential accounting and rental programs.
* Oversee and document processes for the financial aspects of unit sales and ensure accurate transaction recording.
* Ensure rental management financial structure, agreements, and processes are set up and current
* Creating a process to review and audit residential rental accounting to ensure compliance with the rental management agreement and accuracy of the Auberge management fee calculation
* Understanding of rental agreements and rental accounting
* Strategic involvement with finance and revenue management to understand and enhance rental revenue management


Board Communication \& Reporting

* Work with the property team with Boards of Directors when home office engagement is necessary.
* Attend financial meetings with Boards of Directors when support of material residential financials issues or Auberge Brand programs is required. Attendance should not be regular in nature.


Budgeting \& Financial Planning Oversight

* Manage the annual budget timeline and schedules.
* Partner with Directors of Finance and Corporate Finance Operations to ensure hotel and residential budgets and allocations balance and tie between the multiple entities.
* Develop tools, templates, and processes that support a global structure and provide consistency to include validation of residential rental revenue and calculation of residential owner payments.
* Review property operating and capital budgets for accuracy and alignment.
* Validate management fee calculations and exclusions.
* Oversee homeowner assessment billing process for accuracy and compliance.


Training, Policies \& Team Development

* Develop relevant training programs for property finance teams.
* Collaborate with the Regional Director of Finance to onboard and familiarize Directors of Finance and Finance Managers with residential accounting policies and procedures.
* Conduct training on residential accounting policies, procedures, and month\-end processes.
* Lead monthly residential finance meetings with internal stakeholders.


Leadership \& Talent Acquisition

* Provide leadership and mentorship to residential property finance teams.
* Participate in interviews for Directors of Finance and other positions that interface with Residential Finance.
* Participate in interviews with Directors of Finance where residential finance exists or could be added in the future.
* Support strategic workforce planning and succession planning within residential finance.
* Leads global residential financial strategy and identifies opportunities to drive excellence, consistency, and support scaling of new properties.


Pre\-Opening Budget Planning and Critical Path

* Develop and maintain the master residential finance critical path
* Provide guidance on the structure of Homeowners Associations (HOAs) and expense allocation.
* Setup rental program accounting processes, as necessary
* Partner with IT and Finance Operations systems team on systems selection and rollout
* Support required local and state jurisdiction financial submissions.
* Monitor and support completion of critical path with property leadership
* Review pre\-opening expenses to ensure categorized properly and initial filings and submissions are completed accurately.
* Partner with consultants, development and operations teams to create and review pre\-opening budgets.

 **Qualifications*** Accounting designation desirable.
* CPA, CMA, or CAI CMCA (Certified Manager of Community Associations) license highly preferred.
* University degree – major in accounting or finance preferred; MBA or graduate degree ideal.
* Extensive financial experience in residential rental programs / HOA / fractional ownership required.
* Five years of experience in leadership roles in hotel accounting \& finance.
* Luxury hotel experience is not required, though strongly preferred.
* Area or Regional multi\-property management experience preferred.


Skills and abilities


Behavioral

* Ability to create, effectively communicate, and implement residential accounting strategies.
* Liaison for Executive Committee and DOFs with respect to Corporate Accounting/Policy related matters.
* Ability to implement new policies and procedures better aligning organizational needs.
* Strong interpersonal and relationship\-building skills to work with cross\-functional teams; to negotiate for resources and influence stakeholders; and to gain acceptance of and commitment to plans, ideas, initiatives, etc.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills to prepare and deliver reports, effective presentations and to facilitate meetings.
* Promoter of a continuous learning environment that creates an atmosphere for professional development opportunities.
* Apply an ethical approach to influence the outcome of situations.
* Serve as a role model for others by demonstrating appropriate business conduct and ethical principles.
* Continuously seek to improve/develop the performance of others and continuously strive to improve his/her own performance.
* Strong problem solving, decision making, conflict resolution and strategic thinking skills.
* Strong leadership and communication skills


Technical


Experience and knowledge of:

* Strong knowledge of US GAAP for Common Interest Realty Associations (“CIRA”) and fund accounting.
* HOA governing documents
* Residential focused financial reporting systems
* Residential focused policies and procedures
* The Uniform System of Accounts for Hotels
* Hotel/Residential and Rental Management Agreements
* PCI Security Compliance rules
* A strong knowledge of generally accepted accounting principles, managing intercompany transactions, and job knowledge of all positions in accounting and purchasing. knowledge of taxation, labor, and legal matters related to hotel and residential community management.
* Ability and knowledge to operate computer equipment and software.
* Ability to read, speak, and write English fluently. Additional language abilities are highly desirable.
* Sound knowledge of financial reporting and accounting rules within the region (GAAP, Tax, Fiscal Policy)
* Solid cost analysis and financial budget management skills
* Strong cash forecasting and management skills
* Ability to develop effective internal controls through creating policies and procedures, talent selection and development, and implementation of appropriate financial technology solutions.
* Understanding of residential and mixed\-use accounting

 **Additional Information**  

About Friedkin:


Friedkin is a privately held family of global brands spanning automotive, entertainment, hospitality, investments, and sports.


Founded in 1969 and headquartered in Houston, Texas, the company is led by Chief Executive Dan Friedkin, and united by a mission to build breakthrough brands that redefine the status quo.


The Friedkin portfolio includes Gulf States Toyota, 30WEST, Accelerated Solutions Group, AS Roma, Ascent Automotive Group, Auberge Collection, Congaree, Copilot Capital, Diamond Creek, Everton Football Club, GSFSGroup, Imperative Entertainment, Legendary Expeditions, NEON, Northside Lexus, Pursuit Sports, The Friedkin Group International, USAL and Westside Lexus. For more information, please visit www.friedkin.com


Auberge Collection LLC is an Equal Opportunity Employer, M/F/D/V. Auberge Collection LLC provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all team members and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Auberge Collection LLC complies with applicable state and local laws governing non\-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

title: Fractional Sales Leader
description:
**Job Requirements**  

**Build solutions. Elevate performance. Thrive at New Home Star!﻿**


At New Home Star, we’re not just a national real estate sales organization \- we're a culture\-first, performance\-driven company redefining how new homes are sold. Recognized as a best place to work and known for our dynamic and empowering environment, we bring strategy, creativity, and collaboration to every aspect of what we do.

  

Our Builder Solutions Group is growing, and with that growth comes a powerful opportunity. We are seeking a dynamic Fractional Sales Leader to serve as an executive\-level driver of strategy, performance, and revenue growth across multiple builder partners. This high\-impact role mirrors the scope and influence of a New Home Star Sales Director, strategically deployed across accounts to elevate results at scale.

  

The Fractional Sales Leader acts as a trusted advisor and performance catalyst, accelerating sales execution, optimizing operations, and unlocking measurable revenue gains for every partner served. This role requires executive presence, disciplined follow\-through, and a relentless focus on outcomes. Success in this position directly strengthens client retention, drives builder performance, and fuels the continued expansion of our Builder Solutions platform.

 **Why Join Us?**


At New Home Star, our number one core value is to provide excellent careers for our people! By joining our team, you will see this value emphasized in all aspects of your career.

* **Winning Culture:** Join a high\-performance team that celebrates collaboration, authenticity, and purpose.
* **Growth\-Oriented:** Be part of a company that invests in your development and gives you the opportunity to grow into future leadership roles.
* **Mission\-Driven Work:** We’re passionate about reshaping the new home sales experience and making a real impact in the industry.
* **Fun and Engaging Environment:** From monthly culture calls to company\-wide celebrations, we believe in creating a place where people love to come to work.
* **Compensation \& Benefits:** This role offers a base salary starting at $60,000, plus a recoverable draw and quarterly performance bonus plan, with final compensation based on experience, performance, and builder client needs. This full\-time role offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with match, paid time off, and more. This is a remote opportunity with a company\-provided laptop.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

* **Executive Builder Partner Leadership:** Lead sales performance for assigned builder partners by executing strategies that drive measurable gains in conversion, follow\-up, appointment setting, and point\-of\-sale execution.
* **Travel \& On\-Site Engagement Execution:** Elevate field performance through strategic visits, analysis, and hands\-on coaching, with travel based on business needs and seasonality.
* **Engagement Deliverables \& Documentation:** Deliver executive recap reports after each engagement, outlining clear recommendations, next steps, and supporting materials that drive measurable improvement.
* **CRM \& Administrative Discipline:** Maintain accurate CRM documentation by logging engagement activity, tracking KPI outcomes, and organizing reports and strategic materials to support revenue visibility and accountability.
* **Revenue Growth \& Portfolio Expansion:** Drive account growth by identifying upsell opportunities, supporting proposals and executive presentations, and participating in referral initiatives.
* **Cross\-Functional Leadership**: Collaborate cross\-functionally to deliver comprehensive builder solutions and share field insights that strengthen products and services.

 **What We’re Looking For**

* **Availability:** Must be available to work a full\-time schedule, Monday through Friday, in the Eastern Time Zone. This is a remote position with travel required as needed. Travel is estimated to be 40–50% depending on assigned engagement scope and seasonal demand.
* **Education:** Bachelor's degree required. Master’s degree preferred.
* **Experience:** Must have experience in new home sales management and training. Collaboration and relationship\-building with new home builder leadership and stakeholders is strongly preferred. Experience working with title companies, in property management, or with mortgages is a plus.
* **Skillset:** Proven ability to analyze performance data and translate insights into strategic, revenue\-driving recommendations, while building trusted relationships with sales teams and builder leadership. Demonstrated success coaching and influencing stakeholders, and confidently representing and promoting services to clients.
* **Qualities:** Confident, proactive leader with strong executive communication skills, a solutions\-oriented mindset, and the ability to collaborate cross\-functionally while operating independently.

 **Applying Instructions**


Ready to take the next step? Visit New Home Star’s Careers Page to explore opportunities and apply: https://www.newhomestar.com/careers

  

Learn more about our award\-winning company on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube: https://www.newhomestar.com/company/about

 *The preceding job description is not a comprehensive list of all duties and responsibilities required of a Fractional Sales Leader.*

 *New Home Star is an equal opportunity employer. Reasonable accommodations will be considered in accordance with applicable law. Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. at the time of application. Visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Employment is contingent upon a background check conducted in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local laws.*

 *New Home Star does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, religion, age, national origin, marital status, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other reason prohibited by law in the provision of employment opportunities and benefits.*

 *Not open to third\-party recruitment agencies.*

title: Controller
description:
**Overview**


Anatta is a global eCommerce agency with operations across India and multiple North American markets. We partner with brands to deliver innovative digital commerce solutions that drive growth and exceptional customer experiences.  

  

We're seeking an experienced Controller to lead our accounting operations and financial reporting as we continue to scale globally. Reporting to the President, you'll be responsible for maintaining financial accuracy, establishing robust controls, and providing strategic financial insights across our multi\-country operations. This role is critical as we strengthen our financial infrastructure to support continued growth.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------


Financial Reporting \& Compliance

* Oversee month\-end, quarter\-end, and year\-end close processes across all entities
* Prepare consolidated financial statements and management reports
* Ensure compliance with US GAAP, Indian Accounting Standards, and local regulations in all operating jurisdictions
* Manage external audit relationships and coordinate annual audits
* Maintain and improve the chart of accounts, accounting policies, and procedures


Financial Planning \& Analysis

* Lead Anatta’s annual budgeting process and quarterly forecasting cycles
* Develop financial models tied to company growth strategy
* Build a financial forecast tied to company growth strategy
* Conduct variance analysis against budget and forecast
* Develop an executive level financial insight dashboard
* Develop financial models for sales, delivery, executives, and etc.
* Develop a workforce planning and capacity planning dashboard
* Perform scenario modeling for market changes and strategic initiatives
* Design cash flow projections, profitability modeling, capacity planning, services margin modeling
* Develop scenario based modeling frameworks
* Define and develop kpi dashboard


Financial Operations

* Oversee all accounting operations
* Manage general ledger, accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, revenue recognition, fixed assets, and etc..
* Manage monthly, quarterly and annual closing process
* Prepare and review financial statements
* Manage accounting software and infrastructure
* Oversee tax filings
* Manage cash flow forecasting
* Monitor operating cash requirements
* Implement and maintain internal controls to safeguard company assets
* Ensure timely and accurate processing of payroll across jurisdictions


Cross\-Border Management

* Manage intercompany transactions and reconciliations between Indian and North American entities
* Navigate foreign currency transactions and hedging strategies
* Coordinate tax compliance across multiple jurisdictions (income tax, GST, sales tax, etc.)
* Handle transfer pricing documentation and compliance requirements


Systems \& Process Improvement

* Optimize accounting systems and implement automation where possible
* Develop and maintain financial dashboards and KPIs for management
* Lead ERP system enhancements or implementations as needed
* Create scalable processes to support business growth


Strategic Partnership

* Partner with the executive management on budgeting, forecasting, and strategic planning
* Provide financial analysis to support business decisions
* Identify cost optimization opportunities and revenue recognition improvements
* Develop board level weekly, monthly, quarterly and annual reporting
* Monitor company profits, margin, and cost

**Requirements**
----------------

* Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; CPA, CA, or CMA strongly preferred
* 7\+ years of progressive accounting experience, with at least 3 years in a Controller or Assistant Controller role
* Experience managing accounting operations across multiple countries/entities
* Strong knowledge of US GAAP; familiarity with Indian Accounting Standards is a plus
* Proven experience with multi\-currency accounting and foreign exchange management
* Experience in professional services, agency, or project\-based business models preferred
* Proficiency with ERP systems (NetSuite, QuickBooks Enterprise, Sage Intacct, or similar)
* Advanced Excel skills and experience with financial reporting tools

**Preferred Skills and Technologies**
-------------------------------------

* eCommerce, digital marketing, or technology agency experience
* Experience working with fractional or part\-time CFOs/financial leadership
* Knowledge of revenue recognition for service\-based businesses (ASC 606\)
* Familiarity with Indian GST, US sales tax, and Canadian tax requirements
* Experience implementing or upgrading accounting systems
* Prior experience managing remote/distributed accounting teams
* Background in fast\-paced, high\-growth environments
* Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities
* Excellent communication skills with ability to explain financial concepts to non\-financial stakeholders
* Leadership and team development capabilities
* Ability to work across time zones and cultures effectively
* Process\-oriented mindset with focus on continuous improvement
* Comfort with ambiguity and ability to build structure in growing organizations
* Strong organizational and project management skills

**What are the perks?**
-----------------------


We do our best to make sure employees are supported in everything they do. We offer a range of benefits and perks:

* Remote first organization
* Full medical, dental, vision, disability, and company paid life insurance
* Employer 401(k) contributions
* Employee assistance program
* Start accrual with 20 days of PTO annually \+ holidays
* Annual winter break: We close the week between Christmas and New Year's so everyone can truly disconnect and recharge
* Paid parental leave
* Company events, happy hours, and retreats
* Budget for continuing education
* Other perks vary by location

**Physical demands requirements**
---------------------------------


Common office/desk environment that involves long periods of time at a stationary desk with extensive computer work that requires repetitive motion of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers.


If this role is filled in the US, the annual salary range is $120,000 USD \- $165,000 USD and is commensurate to several factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to years of experience, skill set / qualifications, and geographical work location considering differences in cost of living, therefore, actual base pay offered may vary.


Compensation Transparency \& Pay Philosophy  

At Anatta Design, we believe in fair and competitive compensation based on location. We post salary ranges in compliance with state requirements for U.S.\-based roles, ensuring transparency for candidates in those regions. If you are applying from outside the U.S., please note that our pay scales are adjusted based on the cost of living and market conditions in each country.
